programme.title: Night Coppers
programme.desc: Brightons night police patrol the towns hectic night life - from a possible suicide up on the cliff, to an urgent manhunt for a violent assailant with a crowbar.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Richard is rushed to Queens, Nottingham, after collapsing suddenly at home and may be going into organ failure. And Colin has sliced his hand open with a Stanley knife.

programme.title: Car S.O.S
programme.desc: Tim and Fuzz are off to Huddersfield to rescue a 1992 VW Corrado. The car belongs to 37-year-old electrician Ashley who has sadly been suffering with ill health.

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The pickers find a mound of motorcycles, but its guarded by booby traps; and a stubborn collector gets in the way of the guys and a mega-warehouse mega-pick.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Gabby hopes that her Sardinian soiree and Dolce Vita dinner will sweeten the deal. And after revealing her nickname on night one, will her guests get a right mouthful?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandBers head to Simon and Vanessas coastal guesthouse in Weymouth, where delightful decor, sumptuous sausages and proper milk await them.

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Rachel and her brothers Spanish ranch opens for guests. David and Lane take drastic action at their French antiques emporium. Stretch and Rachel finish their Portuguese reno.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sarah and Ian met on holiday in Spain and now want a second home in south-east Lanzarote for themselves and their family to enjoy. Jean Johansson shows them five great properties for their budget of £90,000.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Antonia and Ross love the beautiful island of Ibiza. Having holidayed there many times, they now want their own holiday home. Can Lucy Alexander deliver their dream?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Driving instructor Ray Dodd kicks off the competition in Milton Keynes and finds his guests a difficult crowd to please.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Recruitment executive Michelle is determined that her black tie evening will be a winner, but with Ray still reeling from unfavourable feedback on his night, he is out for revenge.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Joke-loving Stuart is bringing the Serengeti to Milton Keynes, hoping to take his guests on the adventure of their lives (and dispel rising tensions) with his Safari-themed dinner party.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Day four in Milton Keynes and choir director Joseph is singing his way through his cooking preparation, putting soul into dishes from his homeland Ghana.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Despite an eventful week, country lover Susan is confident her back-to-basics French food has the edge. Plus Susan reveals some hidden talents, but will an accordion show and origami master class be enough to take prize?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sandy and Andy look for a new home near the beautiful lakes and mountains of Lazio in Italy. Can Danni Menzies find their dream traditional-style house with plenty of space for their £70k?

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil catch up with young couple Wendy and Simon, who were desperate to find their first home in Derby, and their oldest ever house hunters Margot and Henry, in Berkshire. After nearly 50 years in the same home, Margot and Henry wanted to move to something trendy and modern. Or did Henry really want to move home?

programme.title: George Clarkes Building Home
programme.desc: Joel and Nicola restore Joels grandparents 1970s cottage to its former glory, and some!

programme.title: Battle of the Brands
programme.desc: KFC and Nandos go head-to-head as Kate Quilton explores the battle for our taste buds in a fiery look at flavour, fandom and the billion-pound fight to win the chicken wars.

programme.title: Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing
programme.desc: Could you survive being handcuffed to a total stranger 24/7? In a brand-new social experiment, Jonathan Ross challenges 18 brave Brits to stay together as they compete to win a £100k prize.

programme.title: Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing
programme.desc: Could you stay handcuffed to a stranger 24/7? Trad housewife Charlie is hooked to the heavily tattooed Rob. And can Christian hairdresser Nina handle Saras rather chatty nature?

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britains sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the weeks biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Love Bites is a small restaurant in Saugerties, N.Y., run by childhood sweethearts who have trouble separating their work and personal lives; Gordon Ramsay steps in to save both the cafe and the couples engagement.

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Air ambulances bring the emergency room to critically ill or injured people across the country, including a man in cardiac arrest and a woman trampled by a foal.

programme.title: Night Coppers
programme.desc: In the centre of Brighton, a lone officer is violently attacked and urgently needs back-up. And a disorientated drunk going home to bed tries smashing his way into the wrong house.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: A 44-tonne gravel lorry careers off the A697, plunging down an embankment, and Gary must pick up the pieces. In Croydon, Stu has a complex recovery: a bin lorry is stuck in deep mud.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: AJ and Richie are in pursuit of a van speeding through narrow country lanes. Jacko and the team box in a van carrying large quantities of cannabis. Plus, sparks fly when dog handler Justin and police dog Elsa capture two burglars on a building site.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A runaway mugger rams Faulko off the road and puts him in hospital. The team hunts down the driver responsible. Meanwhile Liam locks up a rogue biker with the aid of patience, pepper spray and a heroic passer-by.

programme.desc: Police interceptors pursue a drunk driver whos suspected to be armed with a knife. A cross-county biker gang pays Nottingham a visit. Around 27 motorbikes and quads cause absolute mayhem on the street.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: When Coups and the team get behind a high-performance Mercedes, the driver clocks the cops, goes full throttle up the motorway and careers off road. In Mansfield, Phil Broughton goes undercover to find a van man.

programme.desc: When an angry poker player pulls a gun on him, gambler Zeb Smith is sure hes going to die. However, his life is spared when a preacher takes the fatal bullet. In gratitude, Zeb promises the dying man that hell help build his church.

programme.desc: A 50-ton excavator needs collecting from a farm in north Wales, but road closures hinder plans. Plus, Nick and Oscar must move a life-size metal stegosaurus 25 miles to RHS Garden Wisley.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: In Bradford theres a high-octane chase through a housing estate, a cocky petrol thief tries every trick in the book to wriggle free and Yorkshires back roads have their peace disturbed by two cars carrying gun-toting drug dealers.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Dog handler Darren calls on all his powers of persuasions to try and end a roof-top stand-off. Spike breaks out a drug wipe for a teenage tearaway on two wheels, while Steve is forced to use his pepper spray when a suspect refuses to come quietly.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Chris and the team are in pursuit of a stolen Mazda M3, and the driver disappears into an industrial area. Chris tracker dog Ronny has a twitchy nose and they hope this canine crime fighter will manage to track down the villain.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britains Speeders
programme.desc: PC Will Hunter follows a suspected cloned car, but its driver refuses to do a breath test, and a pursuit hits dangerous speeds through rural villages.

programme.title: Mud Mountain Haulers
programme.desc: The Glovers race to get their wood and machines off Erie Mountain before government road restrictions shut them down for spring. Craigs crew is rushing to get the stranded wood off the landing before break-up drives them out of the bush.

programme.title: Banged up Abroad
programme.desc: A young Londoner heads to Thailand to work in his dads bar, but gets seduced by life in the fast lane, until it all comes crashing down.

programme.title: Extreme Tow Truckers
programme.desc: New South Wales is hit with the heaviest rain in 70 years, which leads to flooding. in Queensland, driver Mackie puts everything on the line while crossing the murky Mary River for a towing mission.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Dougal Brett is depending on the sale of his cattle to pay bills and Marks convoy must cross a series of mountain passes.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: In Northumberland, Kevins winching a tanker out of a muddy ditch. His load is leaning perilously close to an ancient wall and if gets the angle wrong, he might collapse the bricks.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Rescuing a burnt-out low loader and portacabin, a broken-down bus blocking a mini roundabout during the the afternoon school run, and retrieving a car which has careered off the A6.

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Philip, who has a heart condition, collapsed at home and has been vomiting for the last 10 days. Meanwhile, nurse Chantelle and advanced nurse practitioner Cheryl Barnes work together.

programme.title: Skin AandE
programme.desc: Dr Dev Shah struggles to treat a football fan who has a painful ball of fat below his ribcage. Dr Emma McMullen deals with a large liquid-filled neck cyst.

programme.desc: Ellie and Jim, a former DJ, have just got married and set up their brand-new cafe in Benidorm. Also, Zoe and Leeroy are partners in life and in the reggae band they front, and are building up to their first gig after months.

programme.title: Bargain Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Climbing hostel owners Sam and Rich have a coach load of guests about to arrive, giving them a mountain to climb to get everything ready in time. Also, Glaswegian grafter Mags is enjoying some down time.

programme.title: Shoplifters: At War With The Law
programme.desc: At West Orchards, in Coventry, Paige and Cory chase a suspected thief from a new toy shop in the centre. In London, a masked man bursts into a newsagents armed with a rounders bat.

programme.title: Traffic Cops

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: On the lookout for a high-value car thats been taken from a dealership during a test drive. A builders van is taken from a house driveway while its owner pops briefly inside to collect his tools and equipment.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: The traffic cops are alerted when a car is spotted by police cameras being driven with no insurance. PC Andy Swift stops a car after suspecting its not insured. The driver gets straight out and is very aggressive.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Tim is almost mown down by a desperate driver, Adams patience is tested to the limit by a drunken road-hog, and Ange gets spooked while on the lookout for a prowler, and a group of youths gets one over on the interceptors.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A gang of lorry thieves trigger a major manhunt, a young man is busted for heroin possession, a fraudster is taken into custody and a helicopter pursuit ends in an almighty accident.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A petrol thief runs out of gas, the helicopter follows a car thief to the fair and a drunk driver does not have to travel far to be patched up when he crashes into a surgery.

programme.title: Rogue Claimers
programme.desc: Secret filming captures the recovery of a man claiming he can barely walk. An opportunist tries to catch her insurers napping by claiming for an expensive mattress.

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Eden and Cash are navigating the logistics of Remi staying with them long term, only made more complex as John sticks his nose in.

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Mackenzie and Levi are counting down the days until Macs pregnancy has hit the 12-week threshold - and they can finally spread the word of their pregnancy.

programme.title: Street Cops: Catching the Yobs
programme.desc: Kettering v Telford have a bloody history off the pitch so cops are braced for trouble. And officers who spot a drug deal, unearth an Interpol arrest warrant for a criminal.

programme.title: Call the Bailiffs: Time To Pay Up
programme.desc: Agents Sam and Mark enforce a debt of nearly £5000 in unpaid legal fees. In Sussex, Alex seeks to recover a £700 debt from a garage. In London, Vlad has a writ to recover nearly £35,000 in unpaid rent. Agent Lewis is on the hunt for a debtor.

programme.title: Fifty Shades Freed
programme.desc: Believing theyve left behind the shadowy figures from the past, billionaire Christian Grey and his new wife, Anastasia, fully embrace their inextricable connection and shared life of luxury. programme.desc: Romance prevails when Jackie tries to intensify her relationship with Mitch, Matt and Summer deal with their feelings for each other and Hobie falls in love.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: After rekindling her relationship with a former love (John Allen Nelson), C.J. (Pamela Denise Anderson) discovers that he has a serious disease.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: C.J. is pursued by a persistent executive who tries to impress her with his hot-air ballooning skills; Slade returns unexpectedly.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Mitch is kidnapped by a woman who takes him to a fishing boat to treat her wounded husband, an escaped convict.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Hostage Mitch performs surgery on an ex-con; an environmental investigator has a near-fatal accident.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Mitch agonizes over the near-drowning of a boy who lies in a coma.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Mitchs upcoming birthday motivates him to enter an iron-man competition; Matts effectiveness as a lifeguard suffers when he takes a night job.

programme.desc: Sophie and her friends dance like a spider and a whale.

programme.title: Peataichean

programme.title: Na Clangairean
programme.desc: Chan eil an Dràgon-Brot ann an deagh shunnd an-diugh ach dè nì iad mu dhèidhinn? Dràgon-Brot is in a bad mood today but what will they do about it?

programme.title: Na Clangairean
programme.desc: S fìor thoil le Granaidh Clangair a bhith a fighe s a bhith feumail. Granaidh Clanger loves to knit and to be useful.

programme.title: S E Iasg a ThAnnam
programme.desc: A Boxfish explains how she changes colour as she ages, discusses her eating habits and habitat, and demonstrates how she can protect herself from predators.

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Rosemary Ward reads Thig Cò Rium Dhan Sgoil, Calum Cangarù written and illustrated by Emma Chichester Clark and translated into Gaelic by Donald John Macleod.

programme.title: Clann Na Cruinne
programme.desc: Tha bacadh-cainnt air Kana ris an canar stad-cainnt s tha i ag innse dhuinn mu dhèidhinn. Kana has a speech impediment which is called a stutter and she tells us all about it.

programme.title: Flix
programme.desc: Flix is trying to find something that he and his dad, Zeno, can enjoy doing together. Flix is eager to live up to his dads reputation as a Ultimate Outdoor Cat in a scout group called Troop Wildcats.

programme.title: Seonaidh
programme.desc: Bitzer, Shaun and Flock are convinced that a monster is at loose on the farm and hide in the barn.

programme.title: Naidheachdan Neoannach CBBC ALBA
programme.desc: Ellen introduces her personal highlights from the craziest news show on BBC ALBA!

programme.title: Bogaisean is Gumbalan
programme.desc: After being captured by the snikes and put in the mattress mine, Tink, Bounce and Willi bicker about whose escape plan is the best. Above them, the Bottersnikes hold a pageant to crown the ugliest snike on Snike Hill.

programme.title: Slighe Chladach Fiobha
programme.desc: Iagan sees the magnificent Forth Bridges up close, visits the Carlingnose Nature Reserve, Inverkeithing, Dalgety Bay, Aberdour and its Silver Sands, Burntisland and Kirkcaldy. He enjoys the sunshine and gets a few tips from the Aberdour Shinty coach.

programme.title: SpeakGaelic
programme.desc: Joy Dunlop guides viewers through the first steps to inspire people to speak Gaelic. She introduces words and phrases on a variety of everyday topics. A Gaelic learner herself, Joy will support viewers through pronunciation and vocabulary.

programme.title: An La
programme.desc: Local, national and international news from BBC ALBA.

programme.title: Rùm Gu Rùm, Le Erica
programme.desc: In Inverness, an old office on the iconic market brae steps gets transformed into a hairdressers salon.

programme.title: Hebridean Baker: Nordic Islands
programme.desc: Coinneach MacLeod explores the food traditions of Lofoten in Norway. Tha Coinneach MacLeòid a faighinn a-mach mu chultaran a bhidhe ann an Lofoten ann a Nirribhidh.

programme.title: Belladrum
programme.desc: An seinneadair aithnichte Albannach Rianne Downey le seisean acoustic aig Belladrum 2025. Acclaimed Scottish singer Rianne Downey performs an acoustic session at Belladrum.

programme.title: OMC!
programme.desc: Including an unspoken affair of the heart between two rural posties, and the selection process behind Scotlands Croft of The Year.

programme.title: Cumhachd Hitler
programme.desc: Hitler had outwardly emphasised his will for peace, yet once he came to power, he committed his top commanders to a war of extermination that would secure the German Reichs supremacy in Europe and so-called `Living Space in the East.

programme.title: Brunch le Abigail
programme.desc: S e Lite am bracaist as fheàrr le Abigail airson a thoirt air falbh. Ach chan eil càil sìmplidh mun dòigh a th aicese air a dhèanamh. Abigails takeaway breakfast is porridge.

programme.title: Transatlantic Sessions

programme.title: Canals: The Making of a Nation
programme.desc: Liz McIvor tells the story of the early canal builders who struggled with the rugged terrain of Englands Pennine hills. Creating a network of canals in this landscape was an uphill challenge - sometimes literally!

programme.title: Weatherman Walking

programme.desc: Derek travels to the seaside town of New Quay on Cardigan Bay where Dylan and his wife Caitlin lived during the war.

programme.title: The Secret Genius of Plants
programme.desc: Technical developments are enabling people to see the world of plants in ever greater detail, giving insight into their remarkable abilities to adapt and survive.

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: The story of a giant otter cub, who was rescued and is being raised by Brazilian naturalist Carolina Vargas. Sancho has to be bottle-fed, shown how to catch fish, and taught to outsmart the many predators of the Pantanal, the worlds biggest wetland.

programme.title: Tribe
programme.desc: Bruce Parry treks into the jungles of Sarawak, Borneo in search of the last nomadic Penan. The Penan are hunter-gatherers whose forest home is in the process of being cut down around them and whose traditional way of life is about to disappear forever.

programme.title: Bernard Hill Remembers...Boys from the Black Stuff
programme.desc: Actor Bernard Hill, who famously played the character of Yosser Hughes, reflects on the iconic drama series `Boys from the Black Stuff.

programme.title: Boys From the Blackstuff
programme.desc: An astute social commentary about life in recession-hit Britain in the Thatcher era. Chrissie, Dixie, Yosser, Loggo and George sign on at the benefit office before heading off for some short-term work for the builder Malloy. The conversion job turns out to be the future new offices of the DHSS. Unfortunately, DHSS officials Moss and Lawton suspect their scam and are determined to catch them in the act.

programme.title: Boys From the Blackstuff
programme.desc: Dixie Dean, working at night in the port as a security guard, is strongarmed into accepting bribes for allowing the removal of goods under his charge in a docked ship. Chrissie, Loggo and George, the other members of the original gang, meet up prior to Snowy Malones funeral.

programme.title: Boys From the Blackstuff
programme.desc: Chrissie and his wife Angie are driven to despair by money problems and hounding by the Department of Employment investigators.

programme.desc: The staggering investigation into the crypto queen jailed over her £5bn bitcoin stash. And can you help solve the murder of Melanie Hall, who disappeared after a night out in Bath?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Tommy can see the potential of a piece of land backing onto fields in Hitchin in Hertfordshire, while Jacqui looks around a flat in Penge in south east London. Dion explores a terraced house in Nottingham.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross heads to Bryn Y Grog Antiques Centre in Wrexham. With expert help on hand from Thomas Forrester and Stephanie Connell, the teams go head-to-head to see who can buy the most profitable three items to take to auction. Charlie hops over the English border to find out about the history of salt production in Cheshire.

programme.title: Marcus Wareing Simply Provence
programme.desc: Marcus time in Saint-Remy is nearly at an end. During his trip, hes been a sous-chef, been run over by goats, searched for snails, steered a combine harvester, and met some passionate producers. Now, hes been invited to a local farm party.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: With their children fledged, todays couple has decided to move from the busy city of Nottingham to the peace and quiet of the East Devon borders, where Jules Hudson is on hand to help them with a spot of property shopping.

programme.title: Clean It, Fix It
programme.desc: Mark Wright, Maxine Dwyer and Asher Edwards help Alison, a devoted mum, transform her busy one-bedroom flat into a home that feels calm and organised. The space was overwhelmed by toys, limiting Alisons ability to cook, entertain and relax.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Past road trip competitors and comedy icons Jennifer Saunders, Darren Harriott and Phill Jupitus trade punchlines for profits, hunting for antique treasures. No hecklers, please!

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: A twist on the classic quiz show formula, contestants search through the depths of their knowledge in an attempt to score as few points as possible.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mark walks into a risky situation, Kim gets a surprise at the Albert, and theres conflict between Nigels loved ones.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The Bear Ladies revive a 1960s teddy with a rock n roll past, Pierro Pozella fixes a camera that saw action in World War II, and Will Kirk restores a garden bench that honours a lost husband.

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: On the Devon coast, presenter Caroline Hawley is collecting seaweed as she remembers a bygone pastime. She learns that Victorian women were discouraged from studying botany, so they gathered seaweed specimens to take home, press and dry. A few of these amateur naturalists even discovered new species, which were named after them. Over in Exeter, two teams dash around a huge indoor antiques fair in the company of their experts, Kate Bliss and Ben Cooper.

programme.desc: A devastated Roisin tries to explain herself to Tommy but her attempts are thwarted by a shock arrival. Meanwhile, Mikey gives Emma some stern but sage advice regarding Drew, Lenny attempts a different approach to get Callum back into school, and Scarlett confides in Lydia about a cheeky dream.

programme.title: Jules and Gregs Wild Swim
programme.desc: From river swims to wild food, Jules and Greg forage, float and feast their way along the River Tweed, before bedding down in Scotlands oldest continuously inhabited house.

programme.desc: Conversation about Scotlands biggest news stories, hosted by Martin Geissler.

programme.title: David Wilsons Crime Files
programme.desc: Criminologist David Wilson probes Scotlands battle with terrorism and discovers how the problem is a lot closer to home than might be imagined. Investigative journalist Fiona Walker heads to Glasgow Airport, the site of one of Scotlands most notorious terrorist attacks. She recounts the events of 30 June 2007 which saw two men drove their jeep into the terminal building.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: After swimming in shark-infested waters, Steve abseils down to a nest of killer bees with a painful outcome. An eerily close encounter with a ragged tooth shark is then followed by getting airborne with the awesome black eagle.

programme.title: Dragons Den
programme.desc: CEO of Refy Beauty, Jenna Meek, returns to the Den. The pitches this time are a phone call-first dating app, a glam makeover to a camping classic, gut-friendly ferments and headphone kits for kids.

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: The candidates are tasked with selling flowers to the public and corporate clients.

programme.title: The Apprentice: Unfinished Business
programme.desc: Together with the weeks newly-fired candidate, Angela Scanlon and her panel will dissect all the drama from that weeks task, providing fans with in-depth analysis and behind the scenes intel.

programme.title: Tell Me Lies
programme.desc: Lucy and Bree attend a professors Christmas party. Lucy makes a rash decision that affects Pippa.

programme.title: Tell Me Lies
programme.sub-title: Dont Struggle Like That, Or I Will Only Love You More
programme.desc: End of semester Christmas celebrations change everything for everyone once again.

programme.title: Dinosaur
programme.desc: After a drunken error, Nina must battle a number of different challenges to try and correct her mistake. However, despite her best efforts, has the damage already been done?

programme.title: Dinosaur
programme.desc: Ninas work event might be just the distraction she needs, but she quickly realises there is more on the line than just winning Shanes fundraising competition.

programme.title: Dinosaur
programme.desc: After so many misunderstandings, Nina knows her true feelings and wants to make things right. Will a family emergency stop her from declaring everything before its too late?

programme.title: RuPauls Drag Race UK vs the World
programme.desc: The fierce Drag Race UK spin-off continues as our international queens fight to impress Mama Ru and do their countries proud. Award-winning comedian Jayde Adams is on hand to help as for one night only the queens form comedy double acts for RuPauls Fringe Festival. But who will have the last laugh? Amandaland star Lucy Punch joins RuPaul on the judging panel alongside Michelle Visage and Graham Norton as another queen sashays away.

programme.title: Lord of the Flies
programme.desc: A violent ambush spells trouble for Piggy, and Ralph finds himself outnumbered.

programme.title: AI Confidential

programme.desc: Revealing the story of the British teenager whose AI girlfriend encouraged him to try to kill Queen Elizabeth II. What happens when humans start having relationships with AI chatbots?

programme.title: This Is BBC TWO
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.

programme.title: Bear Grylls - Wild Reckoning
programme.desc: Cameron wants his dad to grow up and be more of a father figure to him. Can time in the wild help them reconnect - and put an end to Marks silly behaviour?

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.sub-title: Compilations:The Battle of the Pop Stars
programme.desc: From the road trip archives, music legends Peter Andre, Martin Kemp and Sinitta swap the studio for the saleroom, vying to be top of the pops. But who will get the number one spot?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Steve Brown helps a couple wanting space for animals to swap urban Gloucestershire for rural Herefordshire and also visits former escapees who now run a thriving organic farm.

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: The team returns to Okavango Delta to catch up with old friends, lionesses hunt nonstop to feed growing cubs, leopardess Xudum moves on, and the new mum, Lediba, steps into the spotlight. Pobe the cheetah returns with two cubs of her own.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn invites a panel of guests to share their opinions on the newsmaking stories of the day.

programme.title: !mpossible
programme.desc: Thirty players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day.

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz show where players face a question and pick from a board of six rankable answers. Some answers are good, but only one is unbeatable.

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Susan Calman hosts the quiz show where being in control is everything. Who will emerge as the Boss and walk away with the cash? Only the strongest players will survive the game.

programme.title: The Vintage French Farmhouse
programme.desc: In Villeneuve-les-Avignon, James is drawn to a stunning stone sculpture, while Jacqui zones in on a portable French gramophone.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist, Ben Robinson visits the east coast village of Flamborough which sits on a rugged headland sticking out into the North Sea. A largely unassuming village today, it was once effectively cut off from the rest of the country by a five-metre-deep, two-and-a-half-mile long man-made dyke, re-enforced and fortified with local chalk. As Ben discovers when he visits two of the villages most prominent structures, chalk plays a large part in the history of Flamborough.

programme.title: Oceans
programme.desc: The team venture into one of the worlds most hostile environments, the Arctic Ocean, which plays a crucial role in controlling our climate. The team plan to dive beneath the polar ice cap to explore how the ice, the dominant feature of this ocean, is shrinking and how this could affect the rest of the world.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and the team are on the south-east coast, in the seaside town of Bexhill-on-Sea. But theres no time for sunbathing as experts Michael Baggott and David Fletcher have their work cut out digging and delving through bags and boxes looking for those unwanted antiques. David finds a charming art deco childrens clock while Michael may have discovered a painting that could be worth thousands!

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Former players Josh Jones, Sarah Keyworth, Melvin Odoom and Kimberly Wyatt test their general knowledge skills on this weeks Richard Osmans House of Games: Redemption Week.

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Celebrity players Nitin Ganatra, the Rev Kate Bottley and Alex Gray, aka Apollo from Gladiators, face another knowledge battle, led by Vernon Kay and aided by unreliable tour guide Paul Gorton.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The remaining three chefs from Scotland compete over mains and desserts inspired by the British film industry. Who will win, and who must go home?

programme.title: Britains Biggest Warship
programme.desc: HMS Queen Elizabeth is still under construction at Rosyth Dockyard in Scotland as Captain Jerry Kyd and his 700 sailors prepare for dangerous sea trials. Before sailing, the crew have to undergo rigorous fire and flood training.

programme.title: Hostage
programme.desc: In 2012, photojournalists John Cantlie and James Foley chase a scoop in Syria, but their reporting draws the attention of jihadists, and the net closes in on them.

programme.title: We Might Regret This
programme.desc: Freya and Jo revisit the past. Abes stage proposal doesnt go according to plan.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Featuring extensive coverage of the days top national and international news stories, along with explanations about the context and potential aftereffects.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Unspun World with John Simpson
programme.desc: John Simpson brings his expertise to bear on the most important stories. In discussion with the BBCs range of experts across the world, he uses their unique local knowledge to go beyond the headlines to get to the unspun truth about the world.

programme.title: Escaping Utopia
programme.desc: A look into the beginnings of the Gloriavale community and how it evolved from its socialist Christian beginnings to the extreme place it now occupies.

programme.title: Escaping Utopia
programme.desc: Renowned New Zealand journalist Melanie Reid recounts her experiences of going undercover inside Gloriavale in the 1990s, and the important work she did to try and stop the abuse she discovered.

programme.title: Escaping Utopia
programme.desc: The community reels at the loss of its charismatic leader Hopeful Christian, while two former children of Gloriavale expose one of its most shocking secrets.

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: Bitzer has completed all his chores and is at a bit of a loss. Hes delighted when he discovers the barn needs a good tidy-up. Until he comes across a wonky picture that just wont hang straight.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: The world is in jeopardy and the only thing that can save it is a 1,000ft Penfold.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: Twinkle uses her excellent pocket beast card skills when on a quest to rescue the missing Council of Happiness from Grackle the Giant.

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: With Boys new help-for-hire business booming, he overworks his unpaid employee, Dog, without realising. Dog is too loyal to say no, but hes miserable, especially when his next job is walking some mean cats.

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: News magazine keeping young viewers up to date with the latest stories and events happening at home and abroad.

programme.title: Grizzy and The Lemmings
programme.desc: Inspired by a magazine, Grizzy decides to redo the cabins interior design. To make sure everything is perfect, he ousts the Lemmings and their electric train, but the Lemmings discover an underground futuristic premises.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: SpongeBob wants to be the first sponge to dive all the way up to the surface of the ocean.

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: When Agent Obfuscos moustache is stolen, its up to Olive and Otto to get it back.

programme.title: Danny and Mick
programme.desc: Its ice cream for everyone when Danny and Mick take on an ice cream van.

programme.title: Hey You What If?
programme.desc: Is it really possible to teleport? Spoiler alert: it could end in disaster!

programme.title: Bitesize: 9-11 Year Olds
programme.desc: Learn about Carl Linnaeus, animals, plants, microorganisms, vertebrates and invertebrates.

programme.title: Wow Thats Amazing
programme.desc: Owain and Tilly discover how to sleep beneath the stars from the comfort of your bed, feel the pressure of a blast-off battle, and find out that seeing double depends on your point of view!

programme.title: Art Ninja
programme.desc: Whilst looking after Gavs cat and Sarahs guinea pig, Ricky gets bitten and suddenly he can hear the pets talking! Not only that, theyre gossiping about him. He also has to contend with the Grand Master making a kazoo and artist Joan Miro inspiring him to craft a 3D name sculpture, before heading to the Yorkshire Sculpture Park to make animal art really worth talking about!

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall takes a really close look at venom. He is spat at and bitten by scorpions, spiders and snakes, all in his quest to prove that venom is one of the most lethal weapons of the animal kingdom.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: Find out why the human body is mostly made of water as the doctors investigate the hypothalamus, the amazing part of the brain that keeps you hydrated.

programme.title: Absolute Genius With Dick and Dom
programme.desc: Dick and Dom reveal the genius of food expert Elsie Widdowson, who helped to devise war time rationing. Under her diet, Britain was the healthiest it had ever been. Inspired by Widdowson, Dick and Dom come up with their own genius idea, involving some rations, some army cadets and a gruelling assault course.

programme.title: Andy And The Band
programme.desc: Odd Sock Alana needs help with her disobedient French poodle, Monty. Andy and the Band try to train the pesky pup, but their canine class is decidedly dog-eared.

programme.title: Gladiators: Epic Pranks
programme.desc: The Gladiators get their own back on Nitro in an epic prank full of pie pandemonium!

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: After Shellington crashes the Gup-D, he teams up with Kwazii, a moray eel and grouper fish to pick up the pieces.

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: Big Lizard and Cosima create a fantastic water ride when they dislodge an underwater rock blocking a volcanic vent. Its all good fun until they realise the sea is starting to change.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: Brinicle invites Pancake onto his radio show where, but to his friends annoyance, his accidental burps prove a hit. But Pancake discovers that fame as burping guy has its downsides.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: Forest inspectors pay the forest a visit to check its super-happy status. Friendlyface sends the heroes on outlandish quests to guarantee results.

programme.title: This Is CBeebies
programme.desc: Welcome to the CBeebies channel. Programmes start at 6.00am.

programme.title: Tish Tash
programme.desc: When Tish struggles with maths homework and wants to give up, Rosa encourages her and tempts her with a pie after her homework is complete.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing has a brand new Hoppity Voosh lunch box, and Pando has the exact same one - match! At first, its fun to match, but things turn sour when Bing and Pando mix their lunch boxes up and end up with the wrong lunch.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po finds a teddy bear in Teletubbyland. Tinky Winky, Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po excitedly show the teddy to the Tiddlytubbies and they all love it very much.

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Tees a growing monkey and is getting quite heavy, however some days he just wants to be carried, all day.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Follow the adventures of Supertato, the worlds first potato superhero.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing is waiting to sit in the fire engine at the park. Pando and Sula both get a turn, but when it is Bings go there is an emergency and the fire engine has to leave.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Mojo and Bo need to get Felix, Dot and Nans giant pie across the lake at once. But Felix wants the pie, and Dot wants Felix. It will take some creative puzzle-solving!

programme.title: Hey Duggees Squirrel Club
programme.desc: Billy and the Sillies introduce a visit to chicken AandE, the Squirrels build a bug house, Norrie has her chance to shine and the Penguins give facts about whats hot and whats not.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee is working on an inventory of the clubhouses supplies to plan for the future. The Squirrels wonder what the future is like - will Enid still be a cat? Could the clubhouse be in space? Luckily, Duggee has his Future Badge and many theories about the answers to their questions. The Squirrels are transported to a very different-looking future clubhouse, with a very different-sounding robot Duggee.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo make mud pizzas and deliver them to the grown-ups, but their pedal cars wheel keeps falling off.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey is the ringmaster in a game of circus with her friends, but Hercules wants to play his motorcycle game instead. Luckily Bluey has a solution to keep everyone happy.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Ubercorn, Glitch and the Go Jetters all head to the Dwarkadhish Temple in Dwarka, India, for the Holi Festival of Colour. This Hindu celebration involves people throwing a colourful powder called gulal all over one another.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of the Best Bowler
programme.desc: When Peter and Nutkins pine cone bowling rivalry gets out of hand it leads to big trouble...

programme.title: Octonauts: Above and Beyond
programme.desc: The Octonauts help when they discover melting permafrost endangering an ecosystem.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: How much fruit will Five need for a fruit festival, and how can Four help find a solution?

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: The adventures of 26 lively letters who can make words come to life by holding hands. O has fun doubling up and making new and different oo sounds in the words moon, foot and boot. He even gets to hoot like an owl.

programme.title: Tiny Wonders
programme.desc: The Noggle is landing, there must be a Tiny Wonder to explore. The Nogglins discover a tangly place. Itty wants to climb the highest. How will she get everyone back down again?

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster and Tiniest Fluffiest have trained together so they can win Three Legged Race Day, but disaster strikes when Elder Kitten puts them into different teams.

programme.title: Playtime Towers
programme.desc: Bella and Lift enjoy making funny honk noises with tubes. They visit Tincy the Spider, who uses terrific tubes to make music with the Playtimers.

programme.title: Roots and Fruits
programme.desc: Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, its Kiwi performing Do the Kiwi Dance, if he manages to find someone to interview him first.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing and Pando are both tired but insist on one last game of hide-and-seek before bed. Pando finds a great hiding place under the covers of Bings bed but by the time Bing finds him hes fast asleep. Bing is tired too and gets upset when he thinks theres nowhere left for him to sleep, but Flop and Padget find a solution.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say `sheep, and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We see lots of different sheep, a white sheep, a baa-ing sheep, a leaping sheep and a noodle sheep! Dee meets some baby sheep and a flock of shake-it-sheep.

programme.title: Lu and the Bally Bunch
programme.desc: Lu learns that Elodie is visiting France. Wanting to go with her friend, Lu starts packing her bags.

programme.title: Piggy Builders
programme.desc: Keith and Rebecca think theyve broken Freds clock and call the Piggy Builders to fix it before Fred finds out. But the repair turns out to be impossible!

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos watch an artist make his clay sculptures head smaller. The Twirlywoos then have a go and make the whole sculpture smaller until it is smaller than the Twirlywoos.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Down on the river, Justin and his friends learn to paddle a bell boat and have a sing song. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble joins Fisherman Tumble by the water as they go duck spotting.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies stand up straight to see who is the tallest and who is the shortest. Tinky Winky and Po play with the Tiddlytubbies, and the children in Tummy Tales put themselves in height order ready for a photograph to be taken.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome babies Theo, River, Arthur, Reece, Luna and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Giovanna has got a blue bib and blue sunglasses inside her bag. All the parents and babies have blue toys and objects in their bags too.

programme.title: I Spy, You Spy
programme.desc: Elsee plays I spy in a beautiful forest with her new friend Raffaella.

programme.title: Maddie and Triggs
programme.desc: Maddie longs for some excitement during a very boring visit to her Grannys house, but little does she know that going on a musical adventure with Granny is anything but boring.

programme.title: Dylans Playtime Adventures
programme.desc: Dylan the firefighter wants to put out fires and do a big rescue, but when all the firefighters get stuck, who will rescue them?

programme.title: Kiri and Lou
programme.desc: Lou eats too much sugar and runs amok at a poetry recital.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger the Tricarrotops explores the world of the crunchiest era, going on adventures throughout the colourful Vegesaur Valley.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: While camping in the bush, Bluey makes a new friend who doesnt speak the same language.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee has made a lovely pie for everyone to share, but slicing a pie five ways is tricky. The Squirrels try to figure out the best to share equally.

programme.title: The Game Catchers
programme.desc: The Game Catchers arrive on a planet characterized by the presence of pipes with vents, where they meet three anteater-like alien children who explain to them about the characteristics of their civilization.

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Its dressing up time and Timmy gets to play doctor, but he wont take his costume off and insists on treating his class mates as patients. When a mysterious case of spots breaks out, his friends turn to Timmy to find the cure.

programme.title: My First Festivals
programme.desc: Maun celebrates the Hindu festival of colour, Holi. Maun begins by going to the park with his family, where they visit a peacock and see its beautiful plumage. Afterwards, they head to the temple to decorate and make their own Rangoli powder.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Brunsli Biscuits are a traditional Swiss recipe that taste and smell very Christmassy. Tilly adds spices such as ground nutmeg to her chocolate biscuit dough, before cutting it into snowflake shapes. With the help of her sister, Tilly decorates her biscuits with a beautiful white icing. Tillys table has lots of Christmas decorations on it as well as the traditional flower of Switzerland, edelweiss. Lets hope Tillys friends enjoy the taste of the biscuits!

programme.title: Ferne and Rorys Teeny Tiny Creatures
programme.desc: Holly wants to know all there is to know about wriggly earthworms. Ferne visits a worm farm and meets worm expert Ken, and they handle different species of UK worms. Meanwhile, Rory and his son Sandy search the compost bin and the garden for worms.

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: Guide dog Kika and her owner Dr Amit are off to the Sticks n Stuff shop with Amits friend Alana, who is also blind, to help her buy a new white cane.

programme.title: Go Wild
programme.sub-title: Aneeshwar and Lizzie: Brown Bear and Hazel Dormouse
programme.desc: Aneeshwar and Lizzie meet brown bears and a hazel dormouse.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle, Jess, Gramps and Angela help Pepper find a new owner by making a leaflet. Waffle tries to help Mrs Hobbs, too, but causes chaos. Waffle hopes Mrs Hobbs will adopt Pepper.

programme.title: Puffin Rock
programme.desc: Oona and Baba help Mossy gather berries for a Shrew Crew feast.

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: Animated series about a group of potatoes who sing songs in different genres. The waltz is as easy as one, two, three when the Small Potatoes travel to Victorian-era Vienna.

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: Yukee and TapTap spend the night in a tent and are surprised to meet some beautiful, musical fireflies!

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee and the Squirrels are preparing to have a campfire. Duggee is getting it ready and needs the Squirrels to help find lots of wood for it.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Yakka Dee! celebrates children communicating - because talking fills you up with whizz, pop, bang! In this episode, Dee encourages a friend to say cup and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We see lots of different cups and explore how they look and what they do. We see a pink cup, a sippy cup and even a drippy cup full of Dees paint! We enjoy a flower cup, a flutter cup and a cute little cup full of bubbles.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Numberblock Four has organised a beach day and sandcastle competition for the Friendly Fours - the Numberblocks that make up the Four Times Table. To make the beach day better than ever, Forty has built a beach hut for each friend. Now that everyone has their own beach hut, its finally time for their sandcastle competition.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: A simple skipping game turns into a night at the circus as I,G and H find themselves high up on a tightrope, with thrills and spills as they try to find the right way to get back down.

programme.title: Something Special: Were All Friends
programme.desc: Justin has lots of springtime fun with his friends as they visit a farm. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble is very busy spring cleaning the house for Aunt Polly!

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies play choo-choo and follow the Tubby Custard Machine on a Tubby Custard Ride. In Tummy Tales, children follow their nursery teacher to the local playground.

programme.title: Patchwork Pals
programme.desc: Patchwork Seahorse has a problem: all of his water is dripping down a hole! Can Monkey find a way to help him?

programme.title: Raa Raa the Noisy Lion
programme.sub-title: Raa Raa and the Bell Flowers
programme.desc: Raa Raa is waiting for the beautiful bell flowers to open, but can the fidgety lion sit still long enough to see one?

programme.title: Chuggington
programme.desc: Piper must limit playtime when the Trainees are assigned a job.

programme.title: Gigglebiz
programme.desc: Keith Fitt demonstrates his karate skills. Lots of cheeky animals make a big splash when Will Barrow fills up a birdbath in the garden. Roving reporter Gail Force places the final card on an enormous house of cards. Clumsy DIY enthusiast DIY Dan builds a bike shed but the finished result doesnt quite measure up.

programme.title: Graces Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Grace shows off three amazing machines that all help to build things. She hammers in concrete foundations with a pile driving rig, shows off a pedestal crane and drives an electric mini dumpster.

programme.title: The Game Catchers
programme.desc: The Game Catchers land on Recycling Planet to play ball in the hole.

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: The Octonauts venture into a dangerous red-hot lava tube to rescue a teeny tiny water bear who doesnt seem to mind the heat at all!

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of the Grumpy Owl
programme.desc: When Old Brown takes Peters journal, Peter must choose between saving his most precious possession and saving a friend.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: When the Squirrels start talking to each other in the wrong voices, its clear something has gone awry. But Duggee has his voice badge. He knows what to do!

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: Daphne is upset when her sparkly clip-on earrings start to hurt her ears.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: When Evil Pea gets frozen in ice, Corn decides to take over and become Evil Corn.

programme.title: Weasy Family
programme.desc: The Ducklings transfer Weasys mind into the body of a snail - what could go wrong?

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Preston is in costume as everyones favourite character, Dewey Dragon. But he isnt ready for how demanding Deweys fans are - Mojo and Bo need to help him escape the crowd.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo make mud pizzas and deliver them to the grown-ups, but their pedal cars wheel keeps falling off.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: When Mum and Dad have a disagreement, Bluey makes Dad write a love letter to Mum to make up. Now Bluey must find a way to deliver the letter to Mum.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: When Flutters tongue gets stuck to an ice block, the Piris must team up to find a fix.

programme.title: Andys Safari Adventures
programme.desc: When a weaver ant nest arrives damaged, Andy must stick it back together - but how? He jets off in his safari-mobile to Australia, discovering how weaver ants build their nests and what they use to stick the leaves together. Will Andy succeed? And will he get back to Safari World before Mr Hammond notices the other nest was destroyed?

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle is at Mrs Hobbs house until the Brooklyn-Bells return from holiday. He acts like a cat to please her and helpfully washes up his bowl, but accidentally leaves the taps on.

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: Rafis wish to talk to animals helps reunite a lost baby sparrow with its family.

programme.title: JoJo and Gran Gran
programme.desc: When JoJo cant find a new Captain Chloe book to read, Terrence suggests that she has a look in a different area of the library - the poetry section. JoJo finds an interesting pirate poetry book and reads it with Gran Gran. Later at the shop, Jared tells JoJo he likes poetry too, but the poetry he writes is a bit different, its called rap. With Gran Gran on beatbox, Jared performs his own rap.

programme.title: Sarah and Duck
programme.desc: Sarah is keen to complete her sticker album, so sets off to see if her friends can help with her collection.

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: A special star passes over the planet and as it does, the star roses bloom. The star appears, but where are the roses? Could their absence have anything to do with the Skymoos that Tiny and Small hear on the other side of the planet?

programme.title: Beddybyes
programme.desc: MeMo and BaBa learn how some things are bigger and others are smaller.

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.sub-title: Makka Pakkas Trumpet Makes a Funny Noise
programme.desc: Makka Pakkas trumpet is making a funny noise. There is definitely something wrong with it. Igglepiggle and Upsy Daisy go to see Makka Pakka, and help him discover a stone stuck in his trumpet.

programme.title: Cbeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Sam Ryder sings a special bedtime story about people showing their true colours and being proud.

programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay tries to transform El Cantito, a Puerto Rican restaurant, that is struggling to keep the business afloat with malfunctioning equipment, a small staff and pandemic-induced financial strain.

programme.desc: Offering their unmatched satirical insight into current world events, Adam Hills, Josh Widdicombe and Alex Brooke are joined by comedians Jason Manford, Lou Sanders and Bella Hull to help dissect the biggest news stories of the week.

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Presenters Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer mix a cookery show with a chat show, where they cook in the kitchen and are accompanied by the weeks celebrity guest.

programme.title: Our Welsh Chapel Dream
programme.desc: Keith Brymer Jones and his partner Marj throw themselves into the restoration of a derelict chapel in Wales. As they explore their new home, it appears that theyre not alone, the local pigeons have moved in.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil catch up with young couple Wendy and Simon, who were desperate to find their first home in Derby, and their oldest ever house hunters Margot and Henry, in Berkshire. After nearly 50 years in the same home, Margot and Henry wanted to move to something trendy and modern. Or did Henry really want to move home?

programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Neil Delamere in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.

programme.desc: Sam dispatches Carla to get revenge on John Allen Hill - on horseback, and Cliff suffers from pony distress.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray is ready to speak with Ally about the facts of life only to find that Allys interest has changed from where babies come from to the meaning of life.

programme.desc: The last will and testament of former Judge Umakant Verma included the command that his son Shashikant marry one of their servant girls, the much-loved Gauri. The pair do marry, but when Shashikant makes contact with an old flame, trouble arises.

programme.title: Friends

Meanwhile, Monica dates a millionaire, and Joey is cast in a play and clashes with the female lead.

programme.title: Friends

programme.desc: Phoebe is reunited with her former singing partner, Leslie, a failed television commercial jingle writer who wants to use Phoebes signature song `Smelly Cat in an advertisement for cat litter. programme.desc: Ross tries to prevent Rachel from learning his disturbing secret. Meanwhile, Monica, Phoebe, Chandler and Joey spend a night eavesdropping.

programme.desc: Examines what made 30-year-old Michelle Mills murder her boyfriend Eddie Miller on Bonfire night 2012.

programme.title: Interrogation Raw
programme.desc: Shock runs through a community when a wealthy businessman is found murdered in South Carolina.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter
programme.desc: The body of Elsie Jennes if found in the basement of her burning house.

programme.title: Imperfect Murder
programme.desc: Investigate the carefully planned schemes of murderers and potential killers which fell apart, ultimately leading to justice being served.

programme.title: Imperfect Murder
programme.desc: On February 17, 2005, Michael Paul Andrade, a university student with aspirations of becoming a doctor, was tragically murdered in his off-campus apartment in San Antonio, Texas.

programme.title: Accident, Suicide, or Murder
programme.desc: When a Navy officer succumbs to a mysterious illness after months at sea, nothing makes sense, until an autopsy yields a stunning revelation. All roads lead to dead ends, until they zero in on an unlikely target.

programme.title: The First 48
programme.desc: A Somali girl is executed in Minneapolis and a young man is left dead in Tulsa when a house party spirals out of control.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter
programme.desc: A runaway teenager is found dead on a mountain road. An anonymous tip leads the victims best friend into suspicion.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter
programme.desc: A seemingly random attack that left a woman dead is investigated. New evidence and a cryptic note, however, reveal more to the case than initially suspected.

programme.title: Court Cam
programme.desc: A woman sneaks drugs into prison for her boyfriend; a lawyer and attorney square off during an explosive argument; a mother faces her daughters killer.

programme.title: Court Cam
programme.desc: A daring escape goes horribly wrong; an armed judge is caught on camera; a man in prison for a crime he didnt commit seeks justice.

programme.title: Court Cam
programme.sub-title: Court Cam Top Five: Outrageous Escapes
programme.desc: From frightening outbursts to furious judges, Court Cam gives viewers an in-depth examination of some of the most stunning and emotional courtroom moments caught on camera.

programme.title: Court Cam
programme.desc: A look at some of the wildest courtroom moments. A defendant gets upset with a judges decision. A man is physically removed from a hearing. A woman causes a scene when she refuses to follow the rules inside a courthouse; and more.

programme.title: Nightmare in Suburbia
programme.desc: The tragic story of Rachel Moran, who went missing on New Years Day, 2004, sparking a mass police search in Hull which would eventually lead to her brutal killer.

programme.title: Nightmare in Suburbia
programme.desc: When a young mother vanished in Hertfordshire, police suspected shed run off with a lover. When they eventually found her body, they had only one suspect in mind.

programme.title: Nightmare in Suburbia
programme.desc: In 2000, the grizzly remains of a man were found in Sheffield and, with just a clay mould of his decomposed face, police uncovered a chilling tale of deathly greed.

programme.title: Accident, Suicide, or Murder
programme.desc: A devoted husband, military man, and father of three calls the police with a chilling confession: hes shot his wife. What first appears to be a straightforward homicide soon unravels into something far more complex.

programme.title: An Hour to Kill
programme.desc: Summer Inman is a custodian at an Ohio bank working the night shift. On her way home, shes tasered and abducted in a back alley by two men. A state-wide search begins for the young mother and leads investigators to uncover an abusive marriage.

programme.title: An Hour to Kill
programme.desc: Kirk Lankford is with his pregnant wife and young son at The Potters House Church in Hawaii. Kirk leads a band practice with his fellow churchgoers, but has plans immediately after the practice - to dispose of the body he murdered earlier that day.

programme.title: An Hour to Kill
programme.desc: When a housekeeper cleans a Las Vegas hotel room, she finds some irregularities that may shed light on a missing gambler who vanishes with $822 on the poker table, and an unidentified dead body lying in a stairwell lying one floor below her.

programme.title: The First 48
programme.desc: A Somali girl is executed in Minneapolis and a young man is left dead in Tulsa when a house party spirals out of control.

programme.title: Cold Case Files
programme.desc: 23-year-old teacher Irene Garza was a former beauty queen and devout Catholic who vanished the evening before Easter. Residents of her close-knit border town did their best to find her, but her body was found floating in a drainage canal.

programme.title: Accident, Suicide, or Murder
programme.desc: A devoted husband, military man, and father of three calls the police with a chilling confession: hes shot his wife. What first appears to be a straightforward homicide soon unravels into something far more complex.

programme.title: Homicide Squad New Orleans
programme.desc: In the heart of Gentilly, Detective Rayell Johnson is called to investigate the chilling murder of Travis Hobbs, a 42-year-old New Orleans native, gunned down in his truck during a thunderstorm.

programme.title: Ozark Law
programme.desc: Officer Brittany Johnson joins the Lake Ozark PDs ranks and gets a baptism by fire responding to a series of chaotic fights and bloody medical emergencies on The Strip.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter
programme.desc: Lt Joe Kenda spent 23 years in the Colorado Springs Police Department where he amassed a lifetime of memories catching killers and helping solve close to 400 homicide investigations.

programme.title: Britains Darkest Taboos
programme.sub-title: A Twins Story: Our Uncle Raped Us
programme.desc: Identical twin sisters Rachel and Tracy were raped by their uncle John McCallum when they were just 5-years-old. The abuse lasted over a period of eight years until they were 13. In 2012 they broke almost 30 years of silence.

programme.title: Interrogation Raw
programme.desc: In 2012, 26-year-old Timothy Rambo Ramsey is killed in a drive-by shooting, but the investigation yields little evidence, and the case goes cold for six years.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter
programme.desc: A runaway teenager is found dead on a mountain road. An anonymous tip leads the victims best friend into suspicion.

programme.title: Imperfect Murder
programme.desc: Investigate the carefully planned schemes of murderers and potential killers which fell apart, ultimately leading to justice being served.

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Mechanics Will and Gus go in search of iconic British sports cars for under £1000. They find a classic MG F to refurbish, but the car has many minor problems.

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: Burton-on-Trent in Staffordshire plays host to David Dickinson and his team. Some glamorous calendar girls turn up on Alison Chapmans table, and one of Tim Hogarths favourite type of items turns up.

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: Oswestry is the location for David Dickinson and his team as David Ford has his work cut out with a young man who has brought in a set of gold sovereigns and Brenda Haller tries her best to buy a Bakelite telephone.

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: The Toyota Sera is a concept car with gull-wing doors and a 50s-style space age glass roof. Its also extremely rare, and the guys find one thats drivable, but not in the way they need.

programme.title: Container Wars
programme.desc: A new team shakes up the competition, Matthew goes hard after a three-wheel adventure and ping pong balls cause chaos in the yard.

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Ashley challenges Seth to a unique competition in the pawn shop. Should he lose, the world will hear the one thing hes been fighting to keep a secret.

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Les loses his temper when the kids suggest he sells their failing second store. With the familys livelihood at stake, Seth puts a sneaky plan into action.

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and dealers Karen Dalmeny, Ian Towning, Janice Kehoe, and Simon Schneider are in St Albans today, where a collection of vintage car brochures surpasses all expectations.

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson is in Oswestry with dealers David Tupman, Karen Dalmeny, Brenda Haller, and David Ford. Items of interest include a Toby jug, and a leather bullion bag. David Ford has mixed feelings about a set of Irish passports.

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his team of Cheryl Hakeney, Henry Nicholls, Jan Keyne and Mark Stevens travel to Congleton, where a sensuous wall plaque causes a quite a stir in the room.

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and David Ford, Henry Nicholls and Helen Gardiner help the people of Winsford sort their treasures from their trash. They try their best to buy their favourite items, but it is an antique amputation kit that causes a stir today.

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson is joined by his team in Barrow-in-Furness. There is a real surprise for one seller who finds out the true value of her item, and some toy cars that have been lovingly looked after bring out the boy in David.

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David and dealers Mark Stevens, Corrie Jeffrey and David Hakeney are in Oldham, to celebrate the 600th show in the series. Items of interest include a unique set of SAS medals, and a world-famous conductors baton.

programme.title: Container Wars
programme.desc: A container with a shady past lands on the auction block.

programme.title: Container Wars
programme.desc: Unexpected surprises are found in a container from south of the border.

programme.title: Container Wars
programme.desc: A mysterious container is dredged up from the bottom of the sea.

programme.title: Container Wars
programme.desc: A container from Mexico may hold damaged goods, and the bids are high on a unique container.

programme.title: Container Wars
programme.desc: Mio and the Israelis team up on a container from Africa. Matt attacks for a can of Chinese goods. Strange items spark an intense bidding war.

programme.title: Container Wars
programme.desc: Four containers from New York make their way to Long Beach. Bidders are interested in a billionaires unclaimed goods, and a one-of-a-kind auction is held.

programme.title: Container Wars
programme.desc: Bidders battle over super heavyweight freight. A container filled with outdoor adventure equipment holds a surprise.

programme.title: Container Wars
programme.desc: The Israelis go after what they think is vintage clothing. A custom container could land a buyer in jail.

programme.title: Container Wars
programme.desc: Containers bound for the games in Russia miss the boat. A waterlogged container piques everyones interest.

programme.title: Container Wars
programme.desc: A surprise item may send the Israelis in search of major dollars and Jason strikes on a container full of wood and weird gear.

programme.title: Container Wars

programme.title: Container Wars

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his team of dealers of Simon Schneider, Mark Stevens, Michael Hogben and Jan Keyne are in Loughborough in Leicestershire. The Corgi and Dinky toys that pull up in the den are not to be missed and a beautiful diamond ring turns up for Mark.

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his team are in Melton Mowbray. Cheryl Hakeney has her work cut out with a seller who has brought in a silver card case, and James Layte is rather puzzled by a tin-plated tile.

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his team of antique dealers travel to Stockport to give members of the public the chance to put their valuables into auction or accept a cash sum. Brenda Haller encounters a stationary box worth writing home about; a maritime picture leaves David Hakeney awash with wonder, but its a pocket watch and set of sovereigns that turns the den into a gold mine today.

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson travels to Coalville in Leicestershire to help members of the public find diamonds in their rough. programme.title: Hardcore Pawn

programme.desc: Staff fight over whether to buy a load of venomous reptiles, and an incident forces Les to seek a new head of security.

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: Dealers Michael Hogben, Brenda Haller, David Tupman and Simon Schneider are in Stoke-on-Trent. Amongst the items of interest are an Aspreys silver chamberstick, a 1950s Wilson pram and a quirky gaming dice.

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Airing from 2005 to 2009 on NBC, the game show series returns to primetime TV with original host Howie Mandel. A contestant must choose from 26 sealed briefcases containing a marker for various amounts of cash -- from one penny to $1 million. The player then eliminates the remaining 25 cases one by one. The chosen ones are opened and the amount of money inside revealed. After several cases are opened, the player is tempted by the Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for not continuing the game and possibly winning a larger sum of money.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: Hollyoaks

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off: Stand Up To Cancer
programme.desc: James McAvoy, Anne-Marie, David Baddiel and Dame Kelly Holmes make decorative tarts and a savoury technical, and render their spirit animals in cake.

programme.title: Mean Streets
programme.desc: A graphic portrayal of life in the underworld of the Little Italy district in New York City. The episodic narrative deals with the experiences and relationships of a petty hoodlum, his epileptic lover and her unstable cousin who is on the run from a loan shark.

programme.title: Leave Her to Heaven
programme.desc: Riveting film noir melodrama about an unredeemably evil, possessive woman who resorts to murder in order to ensure her husband does not share his love with anyone else.

programme.title: Halls of Montezuma
programme.desc: In the midst of World War II, US Marines are sent on a reconnaissance patrol to a Japanese-held island in the Pacific. The mission is to track down a rocket and capture the Japanese for questioning.

programme.title: Sailor of the King
programme.desc: Andrew `Canada Brown, serving in the WWII Royal Navy, is taken prisoner by the German raider that sinks his ship. The ship then puts in for repairs and Brown delays the ships departure by escaping and sniping at the sailors working on the vessel.

programme.title: Tomahawk
programme.desc: Set during the uprising of the Sioux nation in the late 19th century, when broken treaties and a gold rush saw their tribal territories invaded once again. The film centres on Jim Bridger, a scout at Fort Phil Kearney, from where the 7th Cavalry rides to try and hold the peace, but the brutal slaying of innocents by a sadistic lieutenant sees the Sioux in full cry and only Bridgers cunning can help save the soldiers.

programme.title: The Day the Earth Stood Still
programme.desc: A flying saucer piloted by alien Klaatu, lands in Washington to warn Earth to change its warring ways or risk destruction. Along the way Klaatu finds time to learn more about Earth in the company of Helen Benson and her son.

programme.title: The Creator
programme.desc: As war rages between humanity and artificial intelligence forces, ex-special forces agent Joshua is recruited to find a person who has developed advanced artificial intelligence and a mystical weapon that could finally end the war.

programme.desc: The Austrian Franz Jgersttter, a conscientious objector, refuses to fight for the Nazis in World War II.
