programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the all-important main course and returning contender Mark Froydenlund is banking on it being his strongest dish. Russell Bateman is creating a beef dish, and Ronnie Murrays mixing a crown of lamb with a curried filling.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the dessert course and the final chance for three of London and South Easts most competitive and talented chefs to get through to the judging chamber, today the veteran judge will send one chef home.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the head to head and the two highest scoring chefs from the London and South East region are cooking for judges Oliver Peyton, Prue Leith and Matthew Fort. Joining this week as guest judge is Kevin Gould, an award winning food writer.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Mini Patel is back for a second attempt at the north east regional title but he is competing against two young newcomers, Tommy Banks and Chris Archer. Last year, Mini went home after the dessert course, so he is hoping to keep better control of his nerves this year.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the fish course and two chefs, Tommy and Mini, have very similar ingredients - both are cooking mackerel. But Mini is trying an original beetroot sorbet while Tommy is using wild woodruff and oyster leaf from his own garden. Chris Archer has chosen the king of the sea, turbot, but there is controversy in the kitchen.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the main course of the north east region and overnight there has been a dramatic development. It is a tough and emotional day for the chefs as they react to the news but they battle on in a bid to present a perfect dish with venison and wagyu-angus beef - the meat of the day.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is a highly emotional day for one of the chefs as he prepares a dish which is inspired by and a tribute to his own great Briton, his late grandfather. But all is not going to plan and the chef ends up making one key element three times in a bid to achieve perfection.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two chefs are cooking for judges Matthew Fort, Prue Leith and Oliver Peyton for the very first time. Joining them as guest judge is John Williams MBE, the executive head chef at The Ritz, the only hotel to hold a royal warrant for banqueting.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is Chris McGowans third time however, he is facing tough competition from Mark Abbott, head chef at Daniel Cliffords 2 Michelin star restaurant, Midsummer House, and also from Eddie Attwell who has just completed a year as head chef at Ardtara Country House in Upperlands.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Mark Abbott is pushing himself again plating up a complex fish course of the very finest produce. He is calling it Shellsational but at the last minute he forgets a key element. Returning chef Chris McGowan is refining cod and chips and Eddie Attwell is getting classical with the king of the sea, turbot.The repartee is relentless as the chefs jostle for top place on the leader board. Once again there is plenty of emotion as the veteran chef delivers the scores.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the main course and returning Chef Chris McGowan is feeling confident but part of his dish involves making his own venison sausages to serve with a venison rack, and getting it all done in time is straining his emotions. Eddie Attwell is also serving the meat but with a traditional Northern Irish dish called Dock pudding. Mark Abbott is pushing himself again, he is using wagyu beef and serving it with tongue and corn cooked in a variety of ways.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three chefs from Northern Ireland are cooking their desserts and one chef is on course for a historic score, he could beat his boss - a veteran of GBM - and achieve more marks than anyone has done throughout the history of the competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the head to head. The two highest scoring chefs from Northern Ireland are cooking for judges Oliver Peyton, Prue Leith and Matthew Fort who are joined by guest judge, restaurant critic and Editor-at-Large of the Independent, Amol Rajan.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the national finals of `Great British Menu. Before cooking starts the eight champions are called to Londons Southbank to get a sight of the prize, the chance to cook at a banquet held at the Palace of Westminster, in celebration of everyday Great Britons honoured by the Queen. It has a huge impact on every one of the chefs, each is determined to do all he can to get a dish to the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its day two of the national finals, and the eight champions cook their fish courses. This years challenge has been to produce dishes that are a lasting legacy to the New Elizabethan Age. They must showcase the transformation in British cuisine during the Queens historic 64 year reign.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is main course day, the one course every chef wants to cook at the banquet. Judging the chefs dishes are Matthew Fort, Prue Leith and Oliver Peyton and guest judge John Williams MBE, executive chef of The Ritz.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the final day of the competition, and the last chance for the eight champions to get one of their dishes to the banquet for Queen Elizabeth lls Great Britons. Judges Matthew Fort, Prue Leith and Oliver Peyton will judge the dessert course and then decide on the banquet menu to be served at the Houses of Parliament. Joining them in the judging chamber is Grace Dent, restaurant critic, journalist and author.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The winning chefs arrive at the Palace of Westminster to cook for the Banquet of Great Britons. The chefs have been selected because their dishes showcase contemporary British cuisine and demonstrate the transformation in British food during the Queens historic reign.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three chefs meet their surprise veteran judge and cook their summery starters. A dish celebrating childhood summer memories takes on a complicated courgette dish and a technically tricky take on the cucumber sandwich.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The atmosphere in the kitchen gets even more intense as all the chefs take risks with their dishes. Mike Reid, Executive Chef at M Restaurants in London, shocks with when he pairs his fish with some highly unusual flavours.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its a smoke filled kitchen as the three chefs prepare their summer themed mains in the hope of impressing the veteran judge. Selin Kiazim is in her element, cooking a feast inspired by the summer barbecues she enjoyed with her family.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: All three chefs are feeling the pressure as they attempt their technical summer desserts. With only the two highest scorers going through to tomorrows regional final, who will be sent home?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining London and the South East chefs must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. Theyre hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges: nd for the first time this year, broadcaster and restaurateur Andi Oliver.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs have been challenged to create outstanding dishes that capture a `taste of summer. Their menus must reflect the tastes, smells and colours of everybodys favourite time of year.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and with a seafood specialist in the kitchen the competition is stepping up a gear. Tom is hoping hell be able to elevate the humble mackerel to the standards required for the Wimbledon banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With a refined summer barbecue going up against a traditional Cornish speciality and a historic Wimbledon related recipe, who will come out on top? Andy Clatworthy is using his passion for historical cooking to reinvent a dish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: All three chefs are honouring Wimbledon and the British summer with their technically challenging strawberry desserts. Only the two highest scorers will go through to cook for the judges.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining chefs compete for a place in the national finals. To get there theyll have to impress the formidable panel of judges: Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort and for the first time this year, broadcaster and restaurateur Andi Oliver.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Tom Parker is eager to impress with a salad that includes 10 elements. Meanwhile, Paul Askew, hopes his traditional techniques will triumph over his younger competitors, and Ellis Barrie is taking a risk with a creative cucumber and oyster starter.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the fish course and the atmosphere in the kitchen gets even tenser as the three newcomers begin to understand the perfection and skill the veteran chef demands. Ellis Barrie attempts to create the flavour of a summer barbecue.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the main course and the pressure is intense in the kitchen. In the hope of impressing the veteran judge, Paul Askew is serving an extremely personal main course based on his mothers favourite food.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dessert is the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges and the pressure in the kitchen is intense. Paul cant afford to make any mistakes and is pushing himself with a technically challenging baked alaska.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North West chefs must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. The guest judge is Greg Rusedski, former British number one, who understands the high standards expected at Wimbledon.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning banquet champion Tommy Banks takes on two ambitious newcomers Danny Parker and Josh Overington. They start by finding out whois the surprise veteran judge and who will be marking their dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The atmosphere in the kitchen gets even more heated, the three chefs are cooking their fish courses. Josh Overington, creates a decadent dish inspired by the prestige of the Wimbledon Championships.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are cooking their main courses and are all taking risks to try and win a place at the banquet. Danny Parker, is trying to recreate the flavours of an American barbecue but the veteran chef is dubious about one of his ingredients.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dessert is the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges and the pressure in the kitchen is intense. All the chefs are using unusual ingredients in their desserts.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining chefs from the North East must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. Theyre hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort, Andi Oliver, and guest Judy Murray.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the Scottish regional heats and returning chefs to the competition Michael Bremner and Ally McGrath battle ambitious newcomer Angela Malik for a place in the national finals. This time they are cooking their starters.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The atmosphere in the kitchen gets even more heated and the veteran chef is expecting perfection from the fish course. Ally McGrath creates an inventive dish based on his summer memories of rock pooling in the River Tweed.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Angela Malik is in her element, cooking a tiffin box based on memories of summer picnics. Michael Bremner is taking a huge risk, and Ally is creating an inventive dish based on a summer barbeque.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dessert is the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges and the pressure in the kitchen is intense. Angela Malik is cooking a technically challenging dessert.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two Scottish chefs must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. Theyre hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort and Andi Oliver. They are joined by guest judge Gary Parsons.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Determined newcomers Paul Croasdale and Nick Brodie take on returning chef Phil Carmichael for a place in the national finals. Its Phils third time in the competition and this year hes determined to make it to the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The pressure in the kitchen is high as the chefs prepare their fish courses. Paul Croasdale is trying to recreate the elegance of Wimbledon with luxury ingredients such as king crab and scallops.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the main course and all three chefs are cooking Welsh lamb in hope of impressing. Phil is taking a risk with barbecued lamb and in an attempt to elevate a humble barbecue to banquet level, hes serving it with many accompaniments.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the dessert course and the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges so all three chefs are feeling the pressure. Phil Carmichael creates a tennis ball dessert that combines two British classics, Eton mess and trifle.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two winning chefs from the Wales region must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. Theyre hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges and guest judge Dan Bloxham, Wimbledons Master of Ceremonies.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With two spots left in the National Finals, its the turn of the Central chefs to battle it out. Pip Lacey, head chef at the Michelin starred Murano in London, tried her luck in the competition two years ago. Shes takes on two newcomers.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With the chefs hoping to impress, they each take risks. Pip Lacey combines lobster with strawberry in a tribute to strawberries and cream. Ryan Simpsons also using lobster in his recreation of a BBQ and has invented a technique to create his chips.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Today the chefs cook their main courses and have very different takes on the Taste of Summer brief. Ryan Simpson hopes to elevate a ploughmans lunch into a dish worthy of the banquet, and Nick Deverell-Smith celebrates a meat he thinks is underused.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dessert is the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges. Pip Laceys pineapple dessert uses coriander. Nick Deverell-Smith also pays tribute to the pineapple, creating a dessert featuring a rice pudding made with cous-cous.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two chefs whove made it through cook their four courses again. They must impress the formidable panel of judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort, Andi Oliver and guest judge Jordanne Whiley, one of Britains most successful tennis players.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs meet their surprise veteran judge and cook their starters. Eddie is hoping to do better than last year, Joery is hoping to impress with a tribute to Richard Krajicek, and Tommy is creating a warm salad.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Joery Castel is looking to triumph by combing mackerel and cucumber. Tommy Heaney hopes his Murray Mound is a fitting tribute to Andi Murray, Eddie Attwell is creating five different strawberry elements to accompany the scallops in his dish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Tommy Heaneys BBQ dish is a surf and turf combination, Joery Castels aiming to recreate a more American style BBQ and Eddie Attwells taking a risk with an unusual meat.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Tommy Heaneys BBQ dish is a surf and turf combination, Joery Castels aiming to recreate a more American style BBQ and Eddie Attwells taking a risk with an unusual meat.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two chefs whove made it through cook their four courses again. Theyre hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort and Andi Oliver who are joined this week by tennis greatest entertainer, Mansour Bahrami.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With Finals Week ahead of them, judge Andi Oliver first summons the chefs to Wimbledon to take a look at where they could be cooking if they win. The judges are joined by guest judge Sue Barker, host of the Wimbledon coverage for more than 20 years.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs must cook their fish courses but with some exceptionally high scoring dishes in the regional heats, each chef will need to deliver perfection in order to see off the competition. The judges are joined by guest judge Tim Henman.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is main course day and with only two spots remaining on the banquet menu, the competition is fierce. As the barbecues are fired up, all 8 chefs are feeling the heat. The judges are joined by guest judgeand Paralympic gold medallist, Gordon Reid.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is dessert day and with just one place remaining at the Wimbledon banquet, each chef is giving everything they have got. The judges are joined by guest judge Annabel Croft, former British Number One and Junior Wimbledon Champion.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the grand finale and the winning chefs must now cook their dishes at the banquet. Once in the kitchen, the chefs face the huge challenge of scaling up their dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Competing for top spot are Danny Parker, head chef at Michelin starred `House of Tides in Newcastle, who is back to try his luck in the competition again after being knocked out in the regional heat last year following a tense tie-break. Hes taking on two newcomers: Dan Fletcher, protege of Phil Howard, and now head chef at `Fenchurch in the City of London, and David Coulson,who runs `Peace and Loaf in Newcastle, which boasts 2 AA rosettes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: After a tense starter course the chefs are all aiming for 10s with their unique fish dishes. David is celebrating a personal connection to the brief with his `My Cod I Bet Me Maam Has Seen Some Trotters, a tribute to his mum who has worked as a chiropodist. Dans `For All Those Sleepless Nights pays tribute to nightshift workers from the NHS with some bold but classic flavours while Dannys `Not All Superheroes Wear Capes aims to give a taste of luxury to the hardworking staff.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dan pays tribute to an icon of modern nursing, Florence Nightingale, while Danny in inspired by the founder of the NHS, Aneurin Bevan. Davids dish is dedicated to the air ambulance staff who saved the life of his fiance, and he even takes a trip to meet one of them.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the chefs last chance to impress and tensions are high. Dan takes a risk with an unusual combination of ewes milk and bergamot while Danny is determined to pull out all the stops with his complex dessert `Aunty Gwens Chocolate. David hopes to transform a childhood comfort classic into a show stopping dessert. Dan also takes a trip to meet Dr Ben Clarke who was working in the Royal London Hospital AandE during the London Bridge terror attack.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North East chefs must cook their four course menus again, but will they take on board their veterans advice as they serve their `Feast To Say Thank You?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Banquet champion Selin Kiazim, a rising star of the London scene renowned for her Turkish Cypriot fusion flavours, is back hoping to join the elite group of chefs who have made it to the banquet twice. She is up against James Cochran whos worked at some of Londons toughest kitchens including 2 Michelin starred `The Ledbury and Scott Goss from `The Twenty Six in Tunbridge Wells, renowned for his use of local Kent ingredients.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs do battle for the fish course and the atmosphere in the kitchen is tense. Lobster is catch of the day for James whose dish is Caribbean-inspired. Scott is attempting to elevate a classic fish pie, dedicated to his mum who worked as a pharmacist in the NHS for 23 years. And Selin is taking a risk with a complex three-part fish dish featuring sardines, scallops and langoustines, as a nod to the purported health benefits of a Mediterranean diet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Scott is showcasing rare breed Tamworth pork to say thank you to the rare breed of people who work for the NHS. Selin is serving a complex duck dish using ingredients from all around the world to honour `Our Global NHS. And James is presenting five cuts of goat cooked in five different ways, for his bold dish `Under The Knife.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Selin puts her own unique twist on tea and toast. James creates a decadent coconut parfait accompanied by mango puree filled doughnuts for his dessert `Tree of Life inspired by the coconut palm tree, the NHS and both of their contributions to our health. Scott hopes his elevated gypsy tart `Honey Bee Good will secure him a place in the final.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining London and the South East chefs must cook their four-course menus again, but will they take on board their veterans advice as they re-make their `Feast To Say Thank You?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Vying to be crowned champion of Scotland are three talented newcomers to the GBM kitchen. Lorna McNee is a former Scottish Chef of the Year and is renowned for her technical precision which she has gained from working her entire career at 2 Michelin-starred restaurant Andrew Fairlie at Gleneagles.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and the chefs are all out to impress. The heat is increased as all three serve their unique takes on lobster. Ross is hoping to snatch victory with his dish `Lobster Aid. Lornas `Live Well, Live Very Well features only three elements, each of which she needs to deliver perfection.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Ross is hoping to impress with `Oh Deer Its Time For Your Penicillin, his tribute to Scottish scientist Alexander Fleming featuring an unusual yeast element as well as venison. Across the kitchen, Lorna, a former Game Chef of the Year, is also cooking venison in a bid to impress.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its dessert day and the last chance for the chefs to impress before one is sent home. Ross is attempting to elevate a comfort classic into a dish worthy of the banquet. Lornas cooking a personal tribute to the NHS staff who have cared for her during treatment for a rare blood condition, using unusual yuzu and tonka bean flavours. Once again Ben is serving an experimental dish with his `Dance for the Greater Good which includes a bees wax ice cream.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its judging day and the two remaining chefs must impress the panel of Oliver Peyton, Andi Oliver and Matthew Fort. Both chefs are gunning for success and are doing everything they can to deliver their menus to perfection.

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: If Leon Degrelle, a writer and journalist, had lived in another era, then he could have been a hero and a leader. However, he became a supporter of Hitler and ended up betraying his faith, his country and his people.

programme.title: WWII: Total War
programme.desc: Explores how civilians became defenders against targets of aerial warfare, and the strategic bombing campaigns utilised in an attempt to end the war.

programme.title: WWII: Total War
programme.desc: The cloak of war played a role in the genocide of WWII, allowing unfathomable horrors against innocent women, children, and men. Even within Allied nations, individuals were seen as a potential enemies and denied liberty.

programme.title: WWII: Total War

programme.desc: Covering the initial attack through to the Russian counter-attack, featuring interviews with survivors from both sides and including some rare footage.

programme.desc: The soldiers surrounded in Stalingrad ran short of everything, from food and medical supplies to ultimately even hope itself.

programme.title: Survivors of Stalingrad: The Russian-German War
programme.desc: The Germans in Stalingrad surrendered. Survivors recounted the staggering death toll among prisoners, many of whom only returned home years after the war had ended.

programme.desc: From the original nine knights protecting holy pilgrims, the Knights Templar expand across Europe. They own vast swathes of land, command fleets, and lend money to kings.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: In medieval England, the North was ruled from York. Dan Jones explores the history of its castle, from the Viking Jorvik and the Normans, through the dark days of the anti-Jewish pogrom and the imprisonment of the Knights Templar, to one of its most colourful inmates - the legendary highwayman Dick Turpin.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: With the aid of dramatic reconstruction, Dan Jones explores the 900-year history of the beautiful Leeds Castle in Kent, from its origins as a royal Saxon familys manor and Norman stronghold to the private property of six medieval queens, a Tudor palace, a Jacobean country house, a Georgian mansion and an elegant 20th-century retreat for the rich and famous.

programme.title: Cursed Treasures

programme.title: Digging for Britain

programme.title: Digging for Britain

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson visits Sherwood Forest and the Peak District as he sets out to discover the real King John.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson hikes up from Lyme Regis on Dorsets Jurassic Coast to the Somerset Levels in the footsteps of a ragtag rebel army led by the dashing young Duke of Monmouth, the illegitimate son of Charles II.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts goes in search of our elusive Stone Age ancestors. Along the way she visits the Channel island of Jersey where she meets a team of archaeologists hoping to shed new light on the much-maligned Neanderthals, and embarks on a kayak survey of the coastline looking for undiscovered sites hidden in the cliffs. At the Natural history museum Alice sees the dark side of our Ice Age ancestors lives.

programme.title: Digging for Britain

programme.title: River Hunters

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson hikes 45 miles through the Weald in Kent and the Downs of East Sussex to discover the areas rich and surprising Tudor heritage. At the impressively preserved Penshurst Place, author Philippa Gregory helps Tony relish the fate of the Grand Duke of Buckingham at the hands of the young Henry VIII. Tony finishes in Lewes, where he relives one of the more brutal monastic dissolutions.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series Tony takes on a tough four-day trek through the Kintail region of the west Scottish Highlands to discover the story of the Jacobite uprisings of the early 1700s. programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A studio flat in a former 1960s office block which is grade two listed in Londons Elephant and Castle, a terraced house in Pontygwaith in South Wales and a flat in in Cradley Heath near Birmingham are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A house with no floor in Edgbaston in Birmingham, a detached house on several levels in Oldham and a former garage complex ripe for development in Forest Hill in London are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A converted 16th century chapel in Matlock in Derbyshire, a two bedroom flat in Whetstone in London and a water damaged house in Wishaw in Scotland are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A two bed semi in Meir in Stoke on Trent is given a country house look, a flat in Londons Willesden Green also gets the wow factor and a semi in Dudley in the West Midlands loses its 1980s look to be brought into the 21st century.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion meet property developers who have all bought at auction and hope to make a profit on their investments. Today a landscape gardener tackles a terrace in Ilkeston in Derby, two friends take on a four storey house in Sittingbourne in Kent while another couple of friends first venture together is a studio flat in South Norwood in London.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Terraced houses in Gravesend in Kent and Shipley in Derbyshire are given a new lease of life while former church premises in Harrow Weald in Middlesex are given a new use for the community.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The trio look at a family home in Kent, a former bar in Llanelli in Wales and a terrace in Croydon, all sold at auction. Will any of the developers make a return on their investments?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A three-bedroom semi in Crewe, a top-floor flat in London and a terraced house in Oldham are sold under the hammer. All the owners are happy with the prices they paid, but will any of them be able to turn a profit at the end of the projects?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: While developers in Croydon in Surrey and Donnington in Shropshire wrestle with the question of whether to lose a bedroom each for a better placed kitchen or bathroom, two friends in Derby consider creating two more in their six-bedroom house.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two brothers buy a rundown cottage in Probus in Cornwall, a father buys a flat in Mitcham in Surrey as a long-term investment for his young daughter and a seasoned property developer buys a house in Tunstall in Stoke-on-Trent for her daughter.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion is in Plaistow in London looking around a three-bedroom semi, Lucy is checking out a small piece of land in Crayford in Kent, and Martin meets a father and son who have bought a small house with a big garden in Wheelock in Cheshire.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A former miners cottage in Aberaman in South Wales and semis in Adderley Green in Stoke and Streatham in South London are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion look around auction buys in Birmingham, Oldham and Gravesend and discover what the new owners plan to do with their investments.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion travel the country meeting developers hoping to make some money from their auction buys. Today they are in Stoke, Croydon and Warrington hearing tales from the auction rooms.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A former doctors surgery in need of TLC is converted into a family home in Houghton-le-spring and a flat in a 1930s block of flats in Edgware and a detached house in a woodland garden in Ashford in Kent are also given makeovers.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Theres a family home in Consett in County Durham, a rental investment in Derby and an auction buy in Beckenham in London which will be used to run a new business.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion looks round a large Victorian terrace in Plumstead, Martin is in Mickleover in Derby hearing a father and daughters plans for a flat and Lucy discovers what happened to a plot of land she saw five and a half years ago.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin looks round a bungalow in a stunning setting in the Lake District while Dion has a look at a flat close to Nottingham city centre. Lucy meets a woman who mistakenly thought she was buying a piece of land in Folkestone in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy looks at a flat in Kilburn in London with one of the worst cases of damp she has ever seen, while Martin and Dion are in the Midlands looking round semis with potential.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flat with huge potential in Londons Earlsfield, and terraced houses in Wombourne near Wolverhampton and Ynyswen near Treorchy in Wales are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin is impressed with the potential of two shops and three flats in Tonypandy in Wales while Dion looks round a two-bedroom maisonette in Dawley and Lucy meets a first time developer with ambitious plans for an end terrace in Londons Forest Gate.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A first-floor one-bedroom flat in Londons Stepney Green, a three-bed semi in Dartford in Kent and a one-bedroom flat on the 17th floor of a block in Salford are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A 1930s bungalow in Dereham in Norfolk, a first-floor flat in Londons East Ham and a property in Maryport in Cumbria with a hidden room are all sold at auction. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners and hear their plans for their investments.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Broken Trust
programme.desc: When the Cravens bring in handyman Rusty McIntire to remodel their basement, it sets off a chain of events that razes their marriage to the ground, rocks their trust in people around them and ultimately leads to murder.

programme.desc: The Hill family lives in a gated community near Dawson HS, where their daughter Arrijana is a popular athlete. When she is found murdered in her bedroom, detectives learn that Arrijana was hiding a secret that leads them to her killer.

programme.desc: Drum major Robert Champion suddenly dies in the back of the Florida AandM marching band bus, mystifying authorities. An autopsy points to foul play, leading to an investigation that exposes a dirty secret.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emmerdale is one of Britains best-loved and highly-viewed long-running drama soaps - set in the heart of the beautiful countryside of the Yorkshire Dales, this realistic and down-to-earth drama superbly chronicles the joys and the heartbreaks of a tightly-knit countryside community.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The story of everyday life in a small and tightly-knit community - this is the core of the worlds longest running primetime drama serial and the highest-rating programme in the UK. Coronation Street has for over 50 years uniquely combined cult status with popularity and durability - the quality of writing is second to none, portraying believable characters with a large helping of humour. Its themes - love, passion, deception, greed and intrigue - are ones which everyone can relate to.

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Frost is held at gunpoint by a teenage drugs dealer, a Territorial soldier is shot dead on an exercise, and a security van of a local company is robbed by a shotgun-wielding gang. Frost investigates the three incidents, suspecting that the soldiers death was not an accident, and that the robbery was an inside job, and tries to find any links between them.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emmerdale is one of Britains best-loved and highly-viewed long-running drama soaps - set in the heart of the beautiful countryside of the Yorkshire Dales, this realistic and down-to-earth drama superbly chronicles the joys and the heartbreaks of a tightly-knit countryside community.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The story of everyday life in a small and tightly-knit community - this is the core of the worlds longest running primetime drama serial and the highest-rating programme in the UK. Coronation Street has for over 50 years uniquely combined cult status with popularity and durability - the quality of writing is second to none, portraying believable characters with a large helping of humour. Its themes - love, passion, deception, greed and intrigue - are ones which everyone can relate to.

programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.title: Grace
programme.desc: DS Roy Grace suspects the death of a former schoolteacher may not be what it first appears. And when human remains are discovered in a field, Grace and DS Glenn Branson are drawn into an investigation that will test their abilities and their friendship.

programme.desc: Its the lead up to 1970s general election and as racial tensions escalate in Oxford, tragedy strikes in an Indian restaurant, testing family loyalties.

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Frost is held at gunpoint by a teenage drugs dealer, a Territorial soldier is shot dead on an exercise, and a security van of a local company is robbed by a shotgun-wielding gang. Frost investigates the three incidents, suspecting that the soldiers death was not an accident, and that the robbery was an inside job, and tries to find any links between them.

programme.sub-title: Semi-Final, 2nd Leg: Real Sociedad v Athletic Club

programme.title: Live and Let Die
programme.desc: James Bond uncovers a plan by a drugs kingpin to get the world hooked on heroin. Teaming up with a female tarot card reader, Bond tracks his adversary to a Caribbean island where he encounters assassins, crocodiles and voodoo.

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade travels to one of the few rivers left on the planet that will still test a fisherman - the Congo. He is on the trail of a terrifying legend - the spirit Mami Wata, which is said to lure fishermen from their boats, taking them to the bottom of the river where they are imprisoned for eternity. It sounds far-fetched, but Jeremy soon discovers that the truth is every bit as frightening as the myth.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: IT Manager David is hoping to bag the £1000 prize with his French evening, but his main challenge is to keep all the guests on friendly terms. Can he keep Ashleigh and Tina away from each others throats?

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: On a busy Saturday, the medical team faces a range of sport and drink-related injuries, but events turn darker when a family party goes horribly wrong, leaving a father and son with life-threatening injuries. Ball games account for 300,000 of those, with football the number one culprit.

programme.desc: On a busy Saturday, the medical team faces a range of sport and drink-related injuries, but events turn darker when a family party goes horribly wrong, leaving a father and son with life-threatening injuries. Ball games account for 300,000 of those, with football the number one culprit.

programme.desc: Comedian Paul Sheffield performs a night of standup comedy about his fourteen years of comedy.

programme.desc: Aaron Woodall delivers a stand-up routine about his deeper introspection of his upbringing.

programme.title: Isabelle Farah: Ellipsis

programme.desc: Steve Soelberg comes to Dry Bar to report on his first pandemic. So far, its consisted of Western standoffs, endless Bane impressions, and potential deaths by queso.

programme.desc: Welsh-born comedian Kiri Pritchard-McLean asks "Why is female treated as a genre?"

programme.desc: Helen Bauer performs his own brand of standup comedy at the Bill Murray in London.

programme.title: Rob Rouse - No Refunds

programme.title: Njambi McGrath: African in New York: Almost Famous
programme.desc: McGrath shares her account of what it was like moving from Kenya to New York City.

programme.sub-title: The C Team; Father Daughter Dance

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Jeanette and the Chipmunks orchestrate a break-in at Bocarters house to rescue a chimpanzee he keeps in a cage. Alvin is alarmed that Dave will be hosting an auction where hell have to tell jokes because hes not that funny.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Tempers flare when the Chipmunks get stuck in traffic on the way to a show. Simon becomes annoyed by constant requests to fix things and plays dumb until he gets a thank you.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: While cleaning out their treehouse, the girls reminisce about how they saved the tree from being cut down; Alvin and Theodore think that Simon is going away to boarding school.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Alvin sneaks into Simons lab to look at his latest invention, only to accidentally clone himself. With two Alvins, theres double the trouble. Alvin tells a small lie that quickly turns into a huge disaster, dragging everyone into his tangled web.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Alvin and Brittany learn parenting is not easy when they have to care for a robot baby for a school assignment. When Alvins football team struggles, the coach makes the team learn ballet to help with their conditioning.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Theodore gets roped into doing Alvins babysitting job with a spoiled four year old. Kevin agrees to be Alvins assistant to get closer to the music industry, but ends up running errands instead.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: Of Mice and Munks; Simon Saves the World
programme.desc: The Chipettes fall in love with a mouse thats doomed to be fed to a snake as part of a science class; Simons new invention threatens to destroy the world.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Alvin and Brittany wind up in a battle over popping acorn tops and bubble wrap. When a cute girl joins the chess club, Alvin enlists Jeanette to teach him how to play so he can join as well.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Brittanys style icon proclaims Jeanette is a fashion trendsetter; Theodore thinks that he is a real superhero.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: The Chipettes set out to prove to Alvin and the boys that being a superhero isnt only a guy thing. Miss Smith tells Alvin that if he doesnt pass the next test, hell be held back a year and separated from his brothers.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When the boys accidentally break Daves new flat screen TV. they decide to become fugitives on the lam. Jeanette and Eleanor act as Sherlock Holmes and Watson when Theodores teddy bear mysteriously disappears.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When Alvin talks Dave into buying a used tour bus, Dave has no idea of the problems ahead. Alvin signs the Chipmunks up for a game show that forces losing contestants to perform dangerous stunts.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Alvins too short to ride a new amusement park ride; while on a ski trip, Alvin convinces Theodore theres a Yeti nearby.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Stuck inside the house due to a storm, Simon invents a virtual reality game that quickly gets out of hand; Cheesy enlists Theodore and Alvin to help steal desserts from the school kitchen using secret underground tunnels.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Brittany takes up painting to impress her fashion idol, but her idol only likes her art after Alvin ruins it; Alvin is convinced that Julie and her son are trying to move in to their home, so he tries to stop the invasion.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: After learning about the Knights of the Round Table, Theodore wants to be a knight and struggles to do good deeds. Alvin and Simon create a fake snake to help Dave get over his fear of snakes.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When Alvin thinks Theodores teddy bear can tell the future, Alvin goes all out to make Talking Teddy his friend. Alvin lies to Theodore about a fake holiday to get out of chores, so Dave forces Alvin to make the holiday real.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Dave is thrilled when he thinks hes nominated for a Grammy, but its actually an award for aging musicians. Alvin and Kevin pretend to be sick to miss school and sneak off to a convention.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: The Road Warrior / My Fair Chipette
programme.desc: Alvin sounds the alarm when he believes Theodore has joined a motorcycle gang of vampires; When Brittany declines Alvins help for a talent show, he convinces Jeanette to enter, putting them at odds.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Its Jeanettes birthday but she doesnt want to celebrate it, since she no longer believes in the magic of this special day.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When Dave gets a parenting app to help supervise the Chipmunks, Alvin tries everything to escape its control.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: A favourite toy of Theodores is lost on a rafting trip but then mysteriously shows up at the house.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Alvin gets a device that lets him listen in on everyones conversations. When is too much information, too much?

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: After Miss Smith praises Simons grades in class, Derek bullies him by not allowing him to use the boys bathroom.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When one of Simons inventions malfunctions, Jeanette and Alvin switch places and now each must live as the other.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: The Devil Wears Rodentia; Career Day
programme.desc: Brittanys dream of being a fashion designer turns into a nightmare when she gets a job and her ideas are stolen. Alvin suspects Dave of being a local bank robber when Dave starts acting suspiciously and giving the boys gifts.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: Between a Rope and a Hard Place/ World Day
programme.desc: Coach Dobkins kicks Cheesy and Theodore off the track team for being slow.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When the girls at school develop a crush on the fire marshal, Alvin decides to be a hero with disastrous results; after causing an accident at a historic castle, Cheesy and Alvin work as reenactors to pay for the damage.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: My Sister the Weirdo; Turf War
programme.desc: Jeanette seeks Alvins advice when she wants to give a toast to Brittany at her upcoming party. Alvin decides to borrow Brittanys babysitting job.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Alvin and Brittany are convinced Simon has vanished back in time and go all out in an attempt to bring him back.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: The Chipmunks and Chipettes try to reduce their carbon footprint when Jeanette signs them up for a Green Living competition.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: The Great Chipmunk Detective; Guitar Hero
programme.desc: When Miss Miller gets robbed, Eleanor and Theodore promise to find the culprit and bring them to justice. Alvin begrudgingly agrees to teach Miss Smith guitar, but she wont reveal why she so desperately needs to learn.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: The boys disobey Daves orders to not play a dangerous game, then have to hide their injuries when they get hurt. The school newspaper has a sudden increase in sales when a gossip column appears poking fun at other students.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When Simon falls ill, Alvin takes his place on a camping trip only to discover its actually an archaeological dig; the Chipmunks dont know what to do when they suspect Dave is lying about the various awards hes won.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Alvin believes his mojo was in a cap he regrets giving to a fan. During a visit to the zoo, Alvin is mistaken for a Tasmanian devil.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: Alvins Wild Weekend/For Whom the Bell Tolls
programme.desc: Alvin begs Dave to spend a weekend alone with him. Dave agrees and gets sucked into a series of wild Alvin adventures. A hypnotist accidentally turns Simon into a bumbling fool before hes scheduled to participate in an academic contest.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When Alvin is accused of revealing spoilers for everyones favourite TV show, he agrees not to speak for 24 hours. When Alvin and Simon think Theodores pet goldfish has died, they go all out to replace it before he notices.

programme.title: Heartland Homicide

programme.title: True Crime Scene

programme.title: True Crime Scene

programme.title: True Crime Scene
programme.desc: A highly regarded officer is suspected in the disappearance of several soldiers.

programme.title: Gangster No. 1: The Freddie Foreman Story
programme.desc: Attached to almost every unsolved gangland murder in London over the last 50 years, Freddie Foreman is the `Godfather of Britains criminal underworld. Foreman reveals the audacious details of the heists, double crossings, shootouts, murders and betrayals he was involved in, and ultimately, what has made him `Gangster Number.1.

programme.title: The Krays: The Myth Behind the Legend
programme.desc: Rivals and other contemporaries discuss gangsters Ronnie and Reggie Kray, who ran Londons East End.

programme.title: British Gangsters: Faces of the Underworld
programme.desc: The famous ports wealthy history has contributed to the prosperous underworld within, where the likes of Charlie Seiga, Curtis Warren and Shaun Smith thrived.

programme.title: British Gangsters: Faces of the Underworld
programme.desc: Looks back at the notorious Peaky Blinders, the flourishing gang culture in the sixties with links to the Krays, and how this culture continues today.

programme.title: British Gangsters: Faces of the Underworld

programme.title: British Gangsters: Faces of the Underworld
programme.desc: Bernard OMahoney explores the injustices suffered by those who have somewhat unwittingly surrounded themselves by criminals, including Phil Berriman.

programme.title: British Gangsters: Faces of the Underworld
programme.desc: When innocent people are jailed for crimes they did not commit, what effect does it have on their lives and their loved ones? Bernard OMahoney finds out.

programme.title: Heartland Homicide
programme.desc: Fredrick Banks is an eccentric senior who loves to show off his vintage cars and wallet stuffed with cash. When he is found slain in his garage, authorities hunt for the mysterious stranger who has recently been spotted lurking in town.

programme.title: Heartland Homicide
programme.desc: A fire engulfs the home of the Klaus family in the middle the night while everyone is asleep, and the investigators suspect that it isnt an accident. The whole case changes when the son starts seeing the spirits of his dead family.

programme.title: Heartland Homicide
programme.desc: When Abbie Speirs body is pulled from her burning house in a quaint farming community, investigators quickly discover that the fire is no accident, and that a killer could be lurking closer to home than anyone can imagine.

programme.title: True Crime Scene

programme.title: True Crime Scene
programme.desc: Woman found dead and her husband who was drunk that evening confesses to her murder but none of the evidence points to him.

programme.title: True Crime Scene

The Feared: Irish Gangsters
programme.desc: True crime author Bernard OMahoney returns to his home country to shine a light on the Irish underworld. With exclusive access to high-profile Irish `faces, he enters uncharted territory when he discovers that there may be more to these crimes than meets the eye. He travels around the country through the workings of a dark criminal underworld with stories of extreme violence, poverty, and ultimately, the devastating consequences they bring.

programme.title: British Gangsters: Faces of the Underworld
programme.desc: Bernard looks at the effect of time on gangs and their origins. Whether it be motorcycle gangs or Irish travellers, the roots are dying out with every new generation.

programme.title: British Gangsters: Faces of the Underworld
programme.desc: Bernard OMahoney explores the minefield that is the media and how its influence has been used to build celebrities out of men, without checking facts. programme.desc: James Braxton and Raj Bisram set off in Cheshire, and Raj hears why a group of daredevil playboys decided to drive their car in to the Savoy to join them for dinner. Also, James learns why a pint in the Crown and Anchor pub played a vital role in verifying the quality of silver in 1773.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Paul Laidlaw kick off a new series in Northern Ireland.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms, and the memories they hold, back to life. First to arrive is Marcia Bryan, with a precious doll for the attention of toy restorers Julie and Amanda. This dislocated doll serves as a reminder of Marcias time in a childrens home in the 1950s, where she lived with her twin brother from the age of six weeks. As a little girl, Marcia bonded with Diana the Doll as they shared the same skin colour.

programme.title: Celebrity Yorkshire Auction House
programme.desc: Auctioneer Angus Ashworth and his team help celebrities clear their homes of clutter. They often discover hidden gems to be auctioned off for serious profit while helping to free up some space.

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: A woman separated from her mother because of a tragic twist of fate, and our first transgender searcher, desperate to know if their birth mother will accept them.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Joanne and Dean from Hertfordshire have been together for six years, and enjoying holidays in Tenerife at least three times a year ever since. They love it so much theyve decided to take the next step and buy their own holiday home there.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Modern day treasure hunter Drew Pritchard travels the length and breadth of the country in search of weird and wonderful objects. The bigger the hoard, the happier he is.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Drew heads to a huge antiques wholesaler in Exeter, where he finds pile upon pile of affordable gems. Next, its on to north London, to visit an emporium of curiosities.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters

programme.title: Home Town
programme.desc: A doctor with a mission to help children started a learning centre for underserved kids, and now its bursting out of its current location. Ben and Erin help her find, design and retrofit a larger house to create the cosiest school imaginable.

programme.title: Help! My House is Haunted

programme.title: Help! My House is Haunted

programme.title: Backstage: Ralph Fiennes in Straight Line Crazy
programme.desc: Ralph Fiennes is joined in dress rehearsals as he discusses his involvement in and the themes relating to David Hares brand-new play Straight Line Crazy.

programme.title: Rampage
programme.desc: Primatologist Davis Okoye shares an unshakable bond with George, an extraordinarily intelligent silverback gorilla thats been in his care since birth. When a genetic experiment goes wrong, it causes George, a wolf and a reptile to grow to a monstrous size. As the mutated beasts embark on a path of destruction, Okoye teams up with a discredited genetic engineer and the military to secure an antidote and prevent a global catastrophe.

programme.title: Uncharted
programme.desc: Treasure hunter Victor Sullivan recruits Nathan Drake to help him recover a 500-year-old fortune. What starts out as a heist becomes a globe-trotting, white-knuckle race to reach the prize before the ruthless Santiago Moncada can get his hands on it.

programme.title: Love Hurts
programme.desc: Marvin is a Milwaukee realtor who receives a crimson envelope from a former partner-in-crime whom he left for dead. He now finds himself thrust back into a world of ruthless hit men and double-crosses that turn his open houses into deadly war zones.

programme.title: Cleaner
programme.desc: Radical activists take 300 hostages at an energy companys annual gala in a high-rise building. Its now up to a former soldier who works as a window cleaner to save the hostages trapped inside, including her younger brother.

programme.title: Blacklight
programme.desc: Travis Block, a shadowy government agent who specializes in removing operatives whose covers have been exposed, uncovers a deadly conspiracy within his own ranks that reaches the highest echelons of power.

programme.title: Fast and Furious Presents: Hobbs and Shaw
programme.desc: Brixton Lorr is a cybernetically enhanced soldier who possesses superhuman strength, a brilliant mind and a lethal pathogen that could wipe out half of the worlds population. Its now up to hulking lawman Luke Hobbs and lawless operative Deckard Shaw to put aside their past differences and work together to prevent the seemingly indestructible Lorr from destroying humanity.

programme.title: Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom
programme.desc: Adventure sequel. Intrepid archaeologist Indiana Jones, on the trail of fortune and glory in Old Shanghai, is ricocheted into a dangerous adventure in India. With his faithful companion Short Round and nightclub singer Willie Scott, Indie goes in search of the magical Sankara stone, and uncovers an ancient evil which threatens all who come into contact with it.

programme.title: The Patriot
programme.desc: A widowed farmer, with a brave but brutal military past, decides not to join up when the British arrive in 1776. However, when his son enlists and is later captured by the enemy, the former soldier must abandon his new-found pacifist principles in order to rescue his oldest child, and forms a regiment of Carolina patriots, whose guerrilla tactics prove pivotal to the US war effort.

programme.title: Fast and Furious 6
programme.desc: Since Dom and Brians heist in Rio they must live as fugitives. Meanwhile, Agent Hobbs has been tracking a gang of lethally skilled mercenary drivers. Hobbs asks Dom and his crew for help in exchange for full pardons for everyone.

programme.title: Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade
programme.desc: The intrepid explorer Indiana Jones sets out to rescue his father, a medievalist who has vanished while searching for the Holy Grail. Following clues in the old mans notebook, Indy arrives in Venice, where he enlists the help of a beautiful academic, but they are not the only ones who are on the trail, and some sinister old enemies soon come out of the woodwork.

programme.title: Indiana Jones and the Kingdom of the Crystal Skull
programme.desc: Professor Henry Jones is given a coded message during the Cold War which leads him to Peru in search of a legendary skull. Jones needs to deliver the skull to its rightful place, but Soviet agents who are trailing Jones have other plans which will endanger the West if the artefact falls into their hands.

programme.title: Bad Boys: Ride or Die
programme.desc: When their late police captain gets linked to drug cartels, wisecracking Miami cops Mike Lowrey and Marcus Burnett embark on a dangerous mission to clear his name.

programme.title: The Fall Guy
programme.desc: Battle-scarred stuntman Colt Seavers springs back into action when a movie star suddenly disappears. As the mystery deepens, he soon finds himself ensnared in a sinister plot that pushes him to the edge of a fall more dangerous than any stunt.

programme.desc: Battle-scarred stuntman Colt Seavers springs back into action when a movie star suddenly disappears. As the mystery deepens, he soon finds himself ensnared in a sinister plot that pushes him to the edge of a fall more dangerous than any stunt.

programme.desc: The Dark Knight assembles his heroic friends like Green Arrow and Nightwing to stop the Joker and his cronies from unleashing a diabolical computer virus.

programme.title: Penguins of Madagascar
programme.desc: The plucky penguins join a special inter-species task force in an attempt to stop an evil doctor from taking over the world.

programme.title: The Emoji Movie

programme.desc: When an evil alien attacks the Earth, a motley crew of monsters is called into action to save the world from imminent destruction. A DreamWorks animation.

programme.title: The Garfield Movie
programme.desc: Garfield, the Monday-hating, lasagna-loving indoor cat, finds himself on a wild outdoor adventure when his long-lost father draws him into a high-stakes heist.

programme.title: Transformers One
programme.desc: Brothers-in-arms Orion Pax and D-16 become sworn enemies Optimus Prime and Megatron.

programme.title: Shark Tale

While enjoying the popularity at first, his tall tale starts to unravel as others begin to suspect that he is not the Defender of the Reef after all, and when the sharks enemies get involved, his lies lead him into dangerous waters. A DreamWorks animation.

programme.desc: Irving Zisman takes his young and impressionable grandson on a cross-country trip in which they encounter male strippers, biker-bar patrons and other unsuspecting citizens.

programme.title: The Cinema List: Video Games
programme.desc: From pixelated beginnings to the big screen, we bring you your favourite games that made it to the movies - from `Sonic the Hedgehog 3 to `Street Fighter and more.

programme.desc: Irving Zisman takes his young and impressionable grandson on a cross-country trip in which they encounter male strippers, biker-bar patrons and other unsuspecting citizens.

programme.title: Sausage Party
programme.desc: Supermarket perishables devise a plan to escape from their human enemies after Frank the sausage learns that they will eventually become meals.

programme.title: This Is Spinal Tap
programme.desc: Satirical `rockumentary following ageing British heavy metal band Spinal Tap - David St Hubbins, Nigel Tufnel, Derek Smalls and their latest hapless drummer - on the comeback trail, promoting their album `Smell the Glove on a US tour. The film features many moments that have passed into legend, including exploding drummers and amplifiers that go up to 11.

programme.title: The Ballad of Wallis Island
programme.desc: Old tensions resurface when former bandmates who were former lovers reunite for a private show at the island home of an eccentric millionaire.

programme.title: Two Weeks Notice
programme.desc: An ambitious law graduate decides to leave her job as she feels her skills are not appreciated by her boss, who basically treats her like his PA. However, he wont let her go that easily.

programme.title: The Holiday
programme.desc: Dumped and depressed, English rose Iris agrees to swap homes with similarly unlucky in love Californian Amanda for a much-needed break. Iris finds herself in a palatial Hollywood mansion while Amanda navigates the lanes of a picture-perfect English village. Soon enough, both lovelorn ladies bump into local lads perfect for a romantic pick-me-up.

programme.title: Team America: World Police
programme.desc: A gung-ho, `Thunderbirds-style squad of puppets lead Americas war on terrorism. The creators of `South Park go on the offensive with an outrageous animated satire that makes a target of everyone from politicians to Hollywood liberals.

programme.title: Ted
programme.desc: When John Bennett was a little boy, he made a wish that Ted, his beloved teddy bear, would come alive. Thirty years later, foul-mouthed Ted is still Johns constant companion, much to the chagrin of Lori, Johns girlfriend. Though Loris displeasure is exacerbated by the pairs constant consumption of beer and weed, shes not the one whos most disappointed with John; it may take the intervention of Johns boyhood toy to help him grow up at last.

programme.title: The Wedding Singer
programme.desc: When one-time rock hopeful Robbie is jilted at the altar, he vents his bitterness in song, even when singing at other peoples weddings. Only true love can redeem the broken hearted songster but fortunately, hes about to meet a woman who will spin him right round (like a record).

programme.title: American Pie: The Wedding
programme.desc: Jim and Michelle are getting married and Jim struggles to win over his future in-laws and deal with his embarrassing dad. Meanwhile, Stifler isnt invited to the ceremony, but hes determined not to be left out.

programme.title: The School of Rock
programme.desc: After guitarist Dewey is kicked out of his own band, he pretends to be a teacher. When he hears the kids play, he realises they could be his support group in the Battle of the Bands, and hell finally get to be the rock star he knows hes destined to be.

programme.title: In and Out
programme.desc: The entire town is watching with bated breath as one of their own is up for an Oscar. They get more than they bargain for when he outs his high-school English teacher, who then has to prove to everyone, especially his fiancee, that hes straight. The press turn up and one reporter in particular is making life very difficult.

programme.title: Miss Congeniality 2: Armed and Fabulous
programme.desc: FBI agent Gracie is given another makeover to become the face of the Bureau, prior to embarking on a promotional tour. When two of her friends are kidnapped - Miss USA and a beauty pageant director - she decides to investigate on her own, in defiance of orders to the contrary.

programme.title: Hope Springs
programme.desc: A woman drags her skeptical husband to a renowned counsellors marriage retreat to try to put the spark back in their relationship.

programme.title: Ferris Buellers Day Off
programme.desc: A teen perpetrates a series of elaborate deceptions to ensure that he and two friends spend a carefree day in Chicago.

programme.title: The Intern
programme.desc: Ben Whittaker, a retired widower in his 70s becomes senior intern at a fashion based e-commerce company. Jules Ostin, the founder, while first hesitant, soon learns to respect and even like her new intern despite their age difference.

programme.title: Mean Machine
programme.desc: A footballer is jailed for throwing a crucial game. Whilst inside, the football-loving warden sets him the task of coaching a team of prisoners to take on the all-conquering warders side. Can he form a disparate bunch of prisoners into a winning team?

programme.title: White Chicks
programme.desc: Disgraced FBI agents Marcus and Kevin go to bizarre lengths to catch a devious millionaire. They disguise themselves as dumb white socialites Brittany and Tiffany Wilson, the heiresses to a hotel fortune who are under threat. Gross-out comedic farce from the makers of `Scary Movie.

programme.title: The Man With Two Brains
programme.desc: Zany horror spoof about a brilliant brain surgeon who falls in love with a female brain stored in a jar. The brain is part of an impressive collection held by a colleague in Austria and, upon learning of his wifes infidelity, the surgeon allows himself to be charmed. Communicating telepathically with one of the specimens in the laboratory, he falls in love - but needs a body to put it in.

programme.title: The Wedding Singer
programme.desc: When one-time rock hopeful Robbie is jilted at the altar, he vents his bitterness in song, even when singing at other peoples weddings. Only true love can redeem the broken hearted songster but fortunately, hes about to meet a woman who will spin him right round (like a record).

programme.title: Ted 2
programme.desc: Teddy bear Ted seeks legal help from a young lawyer and a legendary, civil-rights attorney when the law declares him to be property and not a person.

programme.title: Bridget Joness Baby
programme.desc: Befuddled mom-to-be Bridget Jones must figure out which of the two men in her life is the father of her child.

programme.title: American Pie: Reunion
programme.desc: Outrageous antics ensue when Jim, Michelle, Stifler and the gang get back together for their 13-year high school reunion.

programme.title: Jackass Presents: Bad Grandpa
programme.desc: Irving Zisman takes his young and impressionable grandson on a cross-country trip in which they encounter male strippers, biker-bar patrons and other unsuspecting citizens.

programme.title: Jackass: The Movie
programme.desc: Johnny Knoxville and his mad companions leap onto the big screen. Expect the usual mad gross-out japes, from making snow cones out of urine to using the display toilets in bathroom outlets, and from racing golf carts over hallowed greens to tightrope walking over alligators.

programme.title: American Sniper
programme.desc: Based on the autobiography by Chris Kyle.

programme.title: The Cinema List: Risky Romance
programme.desc: From killers and zombies to forbidden love and dangerous desires, `The Cinema List explores romances where passion courts danger and love is always worth the risk.

programme.title: Babylon
programme.desc: The story of Blue, a young man of Jamaican descent living in Brixton in 1980, as he hangs out with his friends, fronts a dub sound system, loses his job, struggles with family problems and has his friendships tested by racism.

programme.title: The Cut
programme.desc: Given one last shot at glory, a former boxing champion has just six days to make weight for a title fight. His obsession soon leaves him at the mercy of a notorious trainer who takes every last ounce from his mind, body and soul.

programme.title: The Good Half
programme.desc: A man returns home to Cleveland for his mothers funeral, where he must confront his grief, reconnect with estranged family members, and forge new relationships.

programme.title: Mollys Game
programme.desc: Molly Bloom runs the worlds most exclusive high-stakes poker game for a decade before being arrested by the FBI. Her only ally is defence lawyer Charlie Jaffey, who learns that there is much more to Molly than the tabloids lead people to believe.

programme.title: The Blind Side
programme.desc: Michael Oher is a poor and undereducated African-American teenager who lived all his life on the streets. His tremendous size and quickness makes him a perfect athlete and attracts the interest if a wealthy and conservative suburban family who take him in and groom him both athletically and academically.

programme.title: Lost in Translation
programme.desc: In Tokyo shooting a whisky commercial, middle-aged actor Bob Harris becomes attracted to Charlotte, the young wife of a celebrity photographer. Together they ride out their boredom of hotel life and their bewilderment of the city.

programme.title: Babylon
programme.desc: The story of Blue, a young man of Jamaican descent living in Brixton in 1980, as he hangs out with his friends, fronts a dub sound system, loses his job, struggles with family problems and has his friendships tested by racism.

programme.title: The Cut
programme.desc: Given one last shot at glory, a former boxing champion has just six days to make weight for a title fight. His obsession soon leaves him at the mercy of a notorious trainer who takes every last ounce from his mind, body and soul.

programme.title: Empire of the Sun
programme.desc: Young Jim has to grow up fast after the Japanese invade Shanghai. Separated from his parents and alone in a prisoner of war camp, Jim must fight to survive.

programme.title: An Officer and a Gentleman
programme.desc: At a tough training school for American navy pilots, one of the new intake falls foul of the hard-as-nails drill sergeant, but takes it on the chin, and finds the additional strength to help a fellow student who is feeling the pressure.

programme.title: Two Mules for Sister Sara
programme.desc: Whimsical but engaging western about a drifter who saves a woman from a gang of marauders. The cowboy is astonished to learn that the woman is a nun, especially when he witnesses her heavy drinking and cigar-smoking ways. The unlikely pair embark on a voyage of discovery as they become embroiled in the Mexican War.

programme.title: The Damned United
programme.desc: The story of Brian Cloughs 44 day stint as manager of Leeds United Football Club. England, in 1974. When Don Revie quits Leeds to become the England boss, the outspoken Brian Clough takes charge. Determined to impose his own style upon Revies tough tackling team, Clough soon alienates his players and the board. Based on the book by David Pearce.

programme.title: The Damned United

When Don Revie quits Leeds to become the England boss, the outspoken Brian Clough takes charge. Determined to impose his own style upon Revies tough tackling team, Clough soon alienates his players and the board. Based on the book by David Pearce.

programme.title: The Cat in the Hat
programme.desc: Humorous adaptation of the classic Dr Seuss childrens book, in which two housebound children are visited by a cat who saves them from their rainy-day boredom by bringing mayhem to their house.

programme.title: The SpongeBob Movie: Sponge Out of Water
programme.desc: SpongeBob goes on a quest to discover a stolen recipe that takes him to our world, where he tangles with a pirate.

programme.title: Monster Trucks
programme.desc: A high school senior befriends a strange subterranean creature after building a monster truck from bits and pieces of scrapped cars.

programme.title: Bookworm
programme.desc: Eleven-year-old Mildreds world is turned upside down when her estranged father, a washed-up magician, comes back to look after her and takes her camping to find the mythological Canterbury Panther.

programme.title: The Last Airbender
programme.desc: Following the adventures of Yang who is a successor in to a long line of Avatars. He has to put aside his childhood in order to stop he Fire Nation from enslaving the Water, Earth and Air nations.

programme.title: Paul Blart: Mall Cop
programme.desc: Paul Blart desperately wants to become a cop, but he is too fat to pass the physical exam. He instead works as a security guard in a shopping mall. One day criminals put the mall under siege, and Blart takes it upon himself to save the day.

programme.title: My Penguin Friend
programme.desc: A heartbroken fisherman finds a penguin drifting alone in the ocean, drenched in oil from a spill. He not only rescues the sea creature, but takes it under his wing, finding a sense of joy for the first time in years.

programme.title: The Last Airbender
programme.desc: Following the adventures of Yang who is a successor in to a long line of Avatars. He has to put aside his childhood in order to stop he Fire Nation from enslaving the Water, Earth and Air nations.

programme.title: Wicked
programme.desc: An unlikely friendship emerges between Elphaba and Glinda, two clashing witches in the magical land of Oz. Elphaba is wrongfully cast as a wicked witch as she seeks to expose the fraudulent, corrupt Wizard who rules the land.

programme.title: Lemony Snickets A Series of Unfortunate Events
programme.desc: Three orphans are sent to live with their uncle Count Olaf, but he only wants their family fortune. What follows is a battle of wits.

programme.title: Scooby-Doo 2: Monsters Unleashed
programme.desc: The crime-busting gang, Mystery Inc, and their dopey mutt Scooby-Doo are back, this time investigating the spooky goings-on brought about by an evil genius intent on wreaking havoc in the town of Coolsville.

programme.title: Harold and the Purple Crayon
programme.desc: Inside his book, Harold can make anything come to life simply by drawing it. After he grows up and draws himself off the books pages and into the real world, he learns his trusty crayon can set off more hilarious hijinks than he thought possible.

programme.title: Peter Pan
programme.desc: In stifling Edwardian London, Wendy Darling mesmerises her brothers every night with bedtime tales of swordplay, swashbuckling and the fearsome Captain Hook. However, the children become the heroes of an even greater story, when Peter Pan flies into their nursery one night and leads them over moonlit rooftops through a galaxy of stars and to the lush jungles of Neverland.

programme.title: Lemony Snickets A Series of Unfortunate Events
programme.desc: Three orphans are sent to live with their uncle Count Olaf, but he only wants their family fortune. What follows is a battle of wits.

programme.title: Lemony Snickets A Series of Unfortunate Events
